According to a document from 2026-06-30, I found that the current final route still lists the five requested packets in the non-local closure, while the actual central-extension split packets are already present as a same-family decomposition target. 

run_context

 The sidecar below reroutes the determinant slice through those split actual central-extension packets instead of through MinimalBlockActualCentralExtensionClosers2, which is the file currently pulling the five older extraspecial residuals back into the closure.

I produced the requested sidecar file here:

Download MinimalBlockExtraspecialClosers3.lean

What it does:

Does not prove the old five global axiom signatures directly.
Those signatures are over-broad: for example, minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialNaturalityData is quantified over an arbitrary MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialFamilyData, and the diagonal/polar/radical packets are quantified over an arbitrary equivariant family. The manuscript only constructs these for the coherent scalar central-pushout family K_lambda = K / ker lambda.

Builds the old packet shapes for the coherent actual family.
The file defines:

selfDualityOfActualPhi

naturalityOfActual

diagonalZeroOfActual

polarZeroOfActual

radicalContainmentOfActual

coherentExtraspecialPackets_from_actualCentralExtensionSplit

These use the split actual packets:

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPhiData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionSquareData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionDiagonalData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPolarData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionNaturalityData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionAnnihilationData

minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionRadicalContainmentData

Reruns the integrated finite-count route through the split actual-extension determinant hook.
The new route theorem is:

lean
Q2Presentation.Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_actualCentralExtensionSplit

Expected closure change after integration:

DROP:
  minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSelfDualityData
  minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialNaturalityData
  minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialDiagonalZeroData
  minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialPolarZeroData
  minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialRadicalContainmentData

REPLACE BY:
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPhiData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionSquareData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionDiagonalData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPolarData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionNaturalityData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionAnnihilationData
  minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionRadicalContainmentData

This matches the run context’s current note that Parfit’s actual central-extension split is available and manuscript-shaped; the sidecar makes that split the determinant route instead of retiring it in favor of the five older extraspecial packets. 

run_context

To integrate, place the file at:

Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockExtraspecialClosers3.lean

Then in PresentationCorrect.lean, import it and change the finite-count source from:

lean
Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated G

to:

lean
Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_actualCentralExtensionSplit G

Recommended checks:

Bash
lake env lean Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockExtraspecialClosers3.lean
lake build Q2Presentation.PresentationCorrect

Then audit:

lean
#print axioms Q2Presentation.Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_actualCentralExtensionSplit
#print axioms Q2Presentation.presentation_correct

According to a document from 2026-06-30, Route38 currently builds through finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated38, with the remaining non-local residuals including the Section 7 minimal-block/kernel/head and pushout/determinant packets you listed. 

run_context

 The run context also explicitly identifies Route39’s next slice as kernel §7 least/Frattini/raw/head plus pushout §7 actual scalar pushout/simple-head determinant. 

run_context

I produced a Route39 Section 7 patch and sidecars.

Artifacts

route39_section7.patch

MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute39Proofs.lean

MinimalBlockPushoutRoute39Proofs.lean

IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs39.lean

route39_section7_artifacts.zip

What the patch does

The patch adds three new files:

lean
Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute39Proofs.lean
Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockPushoutRoute39Proofs.lean
Q2Presentation/Induction/IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs39.lean

It also updates:

lean
Q2Presentation.lean
Q2Presentation/PresentationCorrect.lean

so that presentation_correct goes through:

lean
Q2Presentation.Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated39

instead of Route38.

Kernel/head Route39 provider

The new drop-in hook is:

lean
theorem minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelConcreteRoute39
    {p : FramedPair} (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)

It avoids the four namespace-local Route38 simple-head residuals:

lean
MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute38.rawHeadActionNotPointwiseTrivialData
MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute38.rawHeadIrreducibleData
MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute38.rawInvariantHyperplanesContainTData
MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute38.rawLowerInvariantCharacterExistsData

and avoids reassembling the split kernel tower through the current Route38 leaf stack:

lean
minimalBlock_headNontrivialData
minimalBlock_kernelLeastOverChiefData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteFourthPowerData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteFrattiniCentralData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteFrattiniExponentData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteFrattiniNontrivialData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteFrattiniNormalData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteRawActionsData
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteRawObjectsData
minimalBlock_kmodRBridgeData

Instead it routes the same MinimalBlockGroupTowerData through coherent existing closers:

lean
minimalBlock_kernelModuleConcreteProvenanceData_from_kernelFrattiniTowerClosers
minimalBlock_dualInvariantVanishesData_from_simpleHeadScalarClosers
minimalBlock_rawHeadSimplicityActualData_from_simpleHeadScalarClosers
minimalBlock_rawInvariantCharacterData_from_simpleHeadScalarClosers

So the kernel/head closure is lowered from fourteen split Route38 leaves to the coherent actual-kernel and actual-invariant packets already present in the project.

Pushout/determinant Route39 provider

The new drop-in hook is:

lean
theorem minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route39
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadPushoutRouteLowerData D)

It avoids direct dependence on the two current Route38 pushout residuals:

lean
minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutData
minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSimpleHeadDeterminantActualData

by routing through smaller manuscript-shaped providers:

lean
minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutData_from_squarePolarData D
minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSimpleHeadDeterminantActualData_from_semanticClosers
  A.toSemanticData

This is a semantic lowering of the pushout route: the actual scalar family and determinant family are still assembled, but the proof now exposes the square/polar and semantic determinant packets rather than the two broad Route38 residuals.

Integrated Route39 theorem

The integrated sidecar keeps Route38’s candidate §8 and target §8 providers unchanged, then swaps only the Section 7 minimal-block providers:

lean
theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated39 :
    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
      Cardinal.mk (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA
        (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G)))
        =
      Cardinal.mk (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
        (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G)))

The Route39 nonterminal branch uses:

lean
obtain ⟨G⟩ := minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_currentIntegrated39 chief
obtain ⟨Pdet⟩ :=
  minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route39 G.preheadTowerData

and then continues through the existing Route38 fine-realization provider.

Apply and validate

The patch applies cleanly against the attached project tree:

Bash
patch -p1 < route39_section7.patch

Then validate on the Lean v4.28.0/mathlib v4.28.0 project with:

Bash
lake build Q2Presentation.Induction.IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs39 Q2Presentation.PresentationCorrect
lake env lean Q2Presentation/PresentationCorrect.lean

For the axiom audit:

lean
#print axioms Q2Presentation.Induction.minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelConcreteRoute39
#print axioms Q2Presentation.Induction.minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route39
#print axioms Q2Presentation.Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated39
#print axioms Q2Presentation.presentation_correct

What Route41 changes

IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs41.lean is a drop-in successor to Route40. It keeps the Route39 skeleton, but swaps the four Route40 sidecars for Route41 sidecars and changes the final count theorem to:

lean
Q2Presentation.Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated41

The integration patch changes PresentationCorrect.lean from:

lean
Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated40 G

to:

lean
Induction.finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated41 G

and imports IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs41.

Expected closure movement
Candidate §8

Route41 replaces the Route40 candidate semantic leaves:

lean
centralCover_candidateFrattiniCoordinateInverseData
centralCover_candidateFrattiniLiftCoordinateData
centralCover_candidateFrattiniRealizationData
centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarPushoutLinearityData
centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarPushoutValueData

with strictly finer actual-coordinate packets from CandidateScalarFrattiniActualClosers, including pointwise scalar-pushout values, additivity/homogeneity, base/fiber coordinates, curried realization, and base/fiber inverse laws.

The drop-in endpoint is:

lean
centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_scalarFrattiniRoute41
Target §8 / §7

Route41 bypasses the Route40 selected-representative bridge and routes through the lower same-tower Route21 actual bridge:

lean
TargetKmodRRoute41BridgeProofs.centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_route41

Expected old leaves removed from the integrated closure:

lean
TargetKmodRRoute39BridgeProofs.centralCover_targetRoute39LowerQuotientIdentificationData
TargetKmodRRoute40BridgeProofs.centralCover_targetRoute40SelectedRepresentativeActionData

Expected replacement leaves:

lean
TargetKmodRRoute21ActualProofs.centralCover_targetRoute21LowerProjectionBridgeData
TargetKmodRRoute21ActualProofs.centralCover_targetRoute21RepresentativeActionData
Kernel/head §7

Route41 partially advances the kernel slice. It keeps Route40’s concrete least-kernel/Frattini/raw-module route, but lowers the simple-head/scalar part.

Expected old head/scalar leaves removed from the integrated path:

lean
minimalBlock_rawHeadQuotientNoTrivialData
minimalBlock_rawHeadSimplicityActualData
minimalBlock_rawLowerScalarQuotientData

Expected replacement packets:

lean
minimalBlock_rawInvariantFunctionalsKillTData
minimalBlock_rawHeadDualInvariantZeroData
minimalBlock_rawHeadNonzeroStableEqTopData
MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute41.minimalBlock_rawLowerNonzeroInvariantCharacterRoute41Data

The remaining concrete kernel packets from Route40 are intentionally not hidden; those are the next §7 finite-group work packets.

Pushout/determinant §7 / §6

Route41 changes the integrated branch from:

lean
minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route40

to:

lean
minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route41

This routes through MinimalBlockActualDeterminantProofs, replacing the broad Route40 pushout residuals:

lean
minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSimpleHeadDeterminantActualData
minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSquarePolarData

by the narrower square/polar semantic and determinant-split packets: same-family naturality, diagonal vanishing, polar vanishing, and radical containment.

The run_context.md Route40 summary says the previous closure had 24 non-local residuals plus 6 intended local/CFT inputs and 3 standard Lean axioms; this patch is designed to remove the candidate five, target two, pushout two, and three kernel head/scalar residuals from the integrated route, replacing them with strictly finer manuscript-shaped packets while leaving the local/CFT floor untouched.

What Route45 changes:

Candidate §8 now routes through CandidateCurrentPacketSplitClosers plus CandidateExactImageDirectResidualProofs, so the old Route44 broad candidate leaves should be replaced by the current scalar obstruction evaluator and Frattini coordinate/realization packets.

Target §8 now routes through the already-present canonical same-tower Route41 bridge, avoiding the Route44 literal lower action and fixed representative action residual names.

Kernel §7 keeps the coherent Route44 least/collapse/raw tower route, but replaces MinimalBlockKernelConcreteRoute44.rawSimpleHeadSemanticData with the four finer Route21 same-tower simple-head fields.

Pushout/determinant §7 routes through MinimalBlockExtraspecialActualClosers4, replacing the two Route44 broad pushout/determinant leaves with the lower same-family square-polar and determinant semantic packets.

What this Route47 patch does:

Kernel/head Section 7

Adds a Route47 sidecar that replaces the direct Route44.rawSimpleHeadSemanticData dependency in the active integrated route.

Provides two tactical kernel entry points:

minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelConcreteRoute47LowerOnly

count-preserving / conservative path that keeps minimalBlock_lowerGroupModuleData but removes the active raw simple-head aggregate.

minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelConcreteRoute47

more aggressive path through Route45 concrete provenance, intended to attack the lower-group/module packet too.

Pushout/determinant Section 7

Adds a Route47 pushout sidecar that decomposes the current aggregate minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData into Route45 split pushout/determinant packets.

Exposes tactical lemmas for:

actual scalar central pushout,

selected simple-head determinant,

lower pushout route data,

extraspecial annihilation/radical determinant packets.

Integration

Adds IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs47.lean.

Patches PresentationCorrect.lean and root Q2Presentation.lean to import/use Route47.

Leaves Candidate §8, Target §8, and the local/CFT floor untouched.

Turn 3 roadmap: route4 → route5, with a strict axiom-frontier discipline
P0 — Freeze the actual route4 frontier before editing anything

Priority: immediate gate.

Do this first, even if the packet list “looks obvious.” The run context shows the frontier has changed several times across route refinements, and older route2/route3 packet names can be stale.

Create:

lean
-- Q2Presentation/Induction/AxiomFrontierRoute4.lean

#print axioms Q2Presentation.presentation_correct

Proof decomposition:

PhiData:
  well-defined commutator/pairing after quotient by ker λ.

SquareData:
  well-defined square map as a QuadF2 D.M.

DiagonalData:
  qλ(m) = φλ(m,m).

PolarData:
  polar(qλ)(m,m') = φλ(m,m') + φλ(m',m).

NaturalityData:
  equivariance under D.C using D.actR and D.actM.

AnnihilationData:
  qλ|_D.T = 0 and polar(D.T, D.M) = 0.

RadicalContainmentData:
  radical(polar qλ) ≤ D.T.

theorem boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    ¬ ScalarTerminal p → Nonempty (FourierBranchData p) := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨chief⟩ := exists_nonScalarChiefFactor_of_nonScalar p h
  obtain ⟨G⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelModuleSemanticClosers3_noAxiom chief
  obtain ⟨Pdet⟩ :=

  let Fdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData :=
    Pdet.equivariantFamily
  let A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData Fdet :=
    Pdet.annihilationData
  let Rdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData Fdet :=
    Pdet.radicalData
  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet
  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerCurrentIntegrated5
      G h Fdet A Rdet
  exact ⟨fourierBranchData_of B C⟩

theorem boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p) :
    ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
      (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
      framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
      framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated5 p h
  obtain ⟨combine, hA, hQ⟩ :=
    fourier_recursion_step D.W D.dimExp D.zR D.hzR D.children
      D.partialN D.XA D.oA D.XQ D.oQ D.realizeA D.realizeQ
      D.partialA D.partialQ
  exact ⟨D.children, combine, D.small, hA, hQ⟩

theorem boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p ∨
      ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
        (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
        framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
        framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  by_cases h : ScalarTerminal p
  · exact Or.inl (boundaryFramed_scalarTerminal_equal_proven p h)
  · exact Or.inr
      (boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated5 p h)

theorem boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p := by
  suffices H : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ q : FramedPair,
      framedMeasure q = n → framedCountA q = framedCountQ q from
    H (framedMeasure p) p rfl
  intro n
  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with
  | _ n IH =>
    intro q hq
    rcases boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated5 q with hterm |
      ⟨children, combine, hsmall, hA, hQ⟩
    · exact hterm
    · rw [hA, hQ]
      congr 1
      apply List.map_congr_left
      intro c hc
      exact IH (framedMeasure c) (hq ▸ hsmall c hc) c rfl

theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5 :
    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
      Cardinal.mk
        (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) =
      Cardinal.mk
        (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) := by
  intro G _ _ _
  obtain ⟨Frames, Y, hA, hQ⟩ := tameFrameExhaustion_proven G
  rw [hA, hQ]
  congr 1
  funext φ
  exact boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5 (Y φ)

2. Packet 1: split by the actual lower tower

The current structure is already well-shaped:

lean
structure MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData {p : FramedPair}
    (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p) where
  lower : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief
  headNoTrivial :
    MinimalBlockRawHeadQuotientNoTrivialData lower.rawTowerData
  lowerScalarQuotient :
    MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData lower.rawTowerData

The split I would make is not three independent packets for an arbitrary raw tower. It should be one invariant packet indexed by the chosen MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData:

lean
namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

structure MinimalBlockLowerInvariantActualData {p : FramedPair}
    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief) where
  headNoTrivial :
    MinimalBlockRawHeadQuotientNoTrivialData D.rawTowerData
  lowerScalarQuotient :
    MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData D.rawTowerData

namespace MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData

def ofLowerInvariant {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief)
    (I : MinimalBlockLowerInvariantActualData D) :
    MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData chief where
  lower := D
  headNoTrivial := I.headNoTrivial
  lowerScalarQuotient := I.lowerScalarQuotient

end MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData

theorem minimalBlock_groupInvariantActualData_from_lowerInvariantActual
    {p : FramedPair} (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p)
    (hLower : Nonempty (MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief))
    (hInv :
      ∀ D : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief,
        Nonempty (MinimalBlockLowerInvariantActualData D)) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData chief) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := hLower
  obtain ⟨I⟩ := hInv D
  exact ⟨MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData.ofLowerInvariant D I⟩

theorem minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_lowerInvariantActual
    {p : FramedPair} (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p)
    (hLower : Nonempty (MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief))
    (hInv :
      ∀ D : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief,
        Nonempty (MinimalBlockLowerInvariantActualData D)) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_groupInvariantActualData_from_lowerInvariantActual
      chief hLower hInv
  exact ⟨D.groupTowerData⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

Prove it in this order:

Use D.simpleHeadSemanticData / D.headNontrivialData to prove the quotient-head statement for D.rawTowerData. This is the Lean form of lem:simplehead: T = rad M, M/T ≃ V, and (Mᵛ)^C = 0.

Build:

MinimalBlockRawHeadQuotientNoTrivialData D.rawTowerData

from the no-trivial-dual result. The first field says invariant functionals on M kill T; the second says invariant functionals on M ⧸ T are zero.

MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData D.rawTowerData

from the invariant scalar quotient of the lower scalar layers. The nonzero invariant functional must be tied to the actual R/Frattini layer in D.rawTowerData; do not manufacture an arbitrary functional on D.R.

3. Packet 2: re-index the pushout packet by the actual group tower

The current packet is too strong as stated:

axiom minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :

Mathematically, Section 7 constructs the determinant pushout from the actual subgroup tower: K ◁ Y, R=Φ(K), M=K/R, T=(K∩S)/R, and the pushouts K_λ=K/ker λ. It does not supply a determinant/extraspecial family for an arbitrary abstract MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData. The run context explicitly identifies the remaining §7 pushout packet as the coherent determinant-pushout/extraspecial packet tied to the prehead tower, not a free construction for all preheads.

So the route-ready split should be indexed by:

lean
G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief

not by a bare D.

Use the existing semantic pushout declarations as the next lower barrier:

lean
namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

open Q2Presentation.Quadratic

structure MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData {p : FramedPair}
    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief) where
  central :
    MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutSemanticData G.preheadTowerData
  naturality :
    MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutNaturalitySemanticData central
  diagonalZero :
    MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutDiagonalZeroSemanticData central
  polarZero :
    MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutPolarZeroSemanticData central
  radicalContainment :
    MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutRadicalContainmentSemanticData central

namespace MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData

def toRouteLowerData {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief}
    (P : MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData G) :
    MinimalBlockPreheadPushoutRouteLowerData G.preheadTowerData := by
  refine
    { selfDuality := P.central.toSelfDuality
      simpleHeadDeterminant := ?_ }
  refine
    { phi_natural := ?_
      annihilates_on_T := ?_
      radical_subset_T := ?_ }
  · intro lam hlam hinv c m m'
    exact P.naturality.phi_natural lam hlam hinv c m m'
  · intro lam hlam hinv
    exact extraspecial_annihilates G.preheadTowerData.T (P.central.phi lam)
      (P.diagonalZero.diagonal_zero_on_T lam hlam hinv)
      (P.polarZero.symm_polar_zero_on_T lam hlam hinv)
  · intro lam hlam hinv
    exact extraspecial_radical_subset G.preheadTowerData.T (P.central.phi lam)
      (P.radicalContainment.symm_polar_radical_subset_T lam hlam hinv)

end MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData

theorem minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_groupPushoutSemantic
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
    (P : MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData G) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadPushoutRouteLowerData G.preheadTowerData) := by
  exact ⟨P.toRouteLowerData⟩

theorem minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_groupPushoutSemantic
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
    (P : MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData G) :
    ∃ F : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData,
      Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData F) ∧
      Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData F) := by
  let R : MinimalBlockPreheadPushoutRouteLowerData G.preheadTowerData :=
    P.toRouteLowerData
  exact ⟨R.equivariantFamily, ⟨R.annihilationData⟩, ⟨R.radicalData⟩⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

This reassembly is intentionally small: it uses the project’s existing MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutSemanticData, naturality, diagonal-zero, polar-zero, radical-containment structures, and the existing extraspecial_annihilates / extraspecial_radical_subset constructors. The new theorem does not call the old broad minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData.

Proof steps for the provider

Route-ready provider name:

lean
theorem minimalBlock_groupPushoutSemanticRouteData
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData G)

Prove it from the actual subgroup tower:

Define X_R = (Rᵛ)^B = (Rᵛ)^C using G.rawTowerData.R and G.preheadInvariantCharacterData.

For each invariant λ : Module.Dual (ZMod 2) G.preheadTowerData.R, construct the central pushout form from the actual quotient K_λ = K / ker λ. In the current API this lands as:

lean
MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutSemanticData G.preheadTowerData

Prove naturality from conjugation action on the actual central extension:

lean
MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutNaturalitySemanticData central

Prove diagonal-zero and polar-zero on T using the manuscript’s simple-head determinant argument:

lean
MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutDiagonalZeroSemanticData central
MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutPolarZeroSemanticData central

Prove radical containment:

lean
MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutRadicalContainmentSemanticData central

This is the nonsingularity-on-simple-head part of prop:simpleheaddet: the radical in M/T ≃ V is a C-submodule of the simple non-scalar head; if it is all of V, the resulting invariant linear form contradicts the no-trivial-dual / simple-head determinant argument.

4. Route-ready branch replacement

Once the two providers above are available, replace the current branch proof with a theorem that depends only on actual group-tower provenance:

lean
namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

theorem boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_section7Actual
    (p : FramedPair)
    (hGI :
      ∀ {p : FramedPair} (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p),
        Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData chief))
    (hPush :
      ∀ {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
        (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief),
        Nonempty (MinimalBlockGroupPushoutSemanticRouteData G)) :
    ¬ ScalarTerminal p → Nonempty (FourierBranchData p) := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨chief⟩ := exists_nonScalarChiefFactor_of_nonScalar p h
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := hGI chief
  let G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief := D.groupTowerData
  obtain ⟨Pdet⟩ := hPush G
  obtain ⟨Fdet, hA, hR⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_groupPushoutSemantic G Pdet
  obtain ⟨A⟩ := hA
  obtain ⟨Rdet⟩ := hR
  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet
  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerFramedProofs
      G h Fdet A Rdet
  exact ⟨fourierBranchData_of B C⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

5. Concrete proof obligations behind Packet 1

For minimalBlock_lowerInvariantActualData D, the manuscript-shaped obligations are:

D : MinimalBlockLowerGroupModuleData chief
└─ D.rawTowerData gives C, M, T, R, actM, actR

A. Head no-trivial-dual:
   prove MinimalBlockRawHeadQuotientNoTrivialData D.rawTowerData
   from:
   - T is the radical / lower scalar part;
   - M/T is the first non-scalar simple head V;
   - (Mᵛ)^C = 0.

B. Lower scalar quotient:
   prove MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData D.rawTowerData
   from:
   - R is the Frattini / scalar lower quotient attached to K;
   - there is a nonzero C-invariant λ : Rᵛ;
   - over ZMod 2, nonzero λ is surjective.

Do not prove lowerScalarQuotient from Nat.card R > 1 alone. That only says the vector space is nontrivial; it does not produce a C-invariant quotient unless the scalar lower quotient has been tied to the actual lower scalar layer.

6. Concrete proof obligations behind Packet 2

For minimalBlock_groupPushoutSemanticRouteData G, the manuscript-shaped obligations are:

G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief
└─ actual tower:
   Ksub, Rsub=Φ(Ksub), raw M=K/R, T=(K∩S)/R, C=Y/K

A. central:
   construct λ ↦ φ_λ from actual K_λ = K / ker λ.

B. naturality:
   prove φ_λ(c·m,c·m') = φ_λ(m,m') for invariant λ.

C. diagonal zero:
   prove q_λ|_T = 0.

D. polar zero:
   prove b_λ(T,M) = 0.

E. radical containment:
   prove radical(b_λ) ⊆ T.

The existing MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialSimpleHeadDeterminantData then packages these into naturality, annihilation on T, and radical containment for the extraspecial form.

7. Likely mathlib/project gaps

The finite-group gaps are mostly project-local rather than broad mathlib, but a few reusable lemmas are likely worth adding.

Least normal subgroup over a chief factor.
MinimalBlockKernelLeastOverChiefData is stronger than “some minimal K.” You need the lemma that the intersection of all normal K ≤ upper with K ⊔ lower = upper is again such a K, using the chief-factor simplicity. Plain finite-poset minimality is not enough.

Frattini functoriality for finite 2-groups.
You likely need public lemmas around:

lean
frattini K = K^2 * commutator K K
Φ(K) characteristic in K
K normal in Y → Φ(K) normal in Y
K / Φ(K) elementary abelian

The current API has frattini, but the §7 proof needs the R=Φ(K) provenance, not merely a subgroup named R.

Coprime/scalar lower action lemmas.
The proof of lem:simplehead and the determinant step needs finite-action statements of the form:

lower factors scalar/trivial,
first non-scalar factor V simple,
Hom_C(T, Mᵛ) = 0,
invariant functionals on M/T vanish.

These are representation-theory lemmas over ZMod 2, probably easier to prove in project files than to seek in mathlib.

Invariant nonzero functional over ZMod 2 is surjective.
Add the public lemma:

lean
theorem dual_zmod2_surjective_of_ne_zero_public
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module (ZMod 2) V]
    (φ : Module.Dual (ZMod 2) V) (hφ : φ ≠ 0) :
    Function.Surjective φ

Central extension to extraspecial quadratic form.
Reuse the existing project-level Extraspecial.extraspecialForm, extraspecial_annihilates, and extraspecial_radical_subset. Do not re-prove the quadratic-form packaging in the §7 file.

The project plan already flags finite group exact-image/subgroup bookkeeping and finite quadratic/extraspecial APIs as expected gaps, with the remote backend best used for these local finite lemmas.

8. Soundness warnings

The current MinimalBlockGroupInvariantActualData is the right coherence barrier. Splitting it into lower, headNoTrivial, and lowerScalarQuotient without indexing the last two by lower.rawTowerData would be unsound. The snippet above keeps the indexing.

The current minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) is too strong for the manuscript proof. The manuscript constructs the determinant pushout from the actual K/R tower. A bare prehead tower does not remember the literal central extension K_λ=K/ker λ, so proving the theorem for arbitrary D risks either an opaque arbitrary witness or an interface stronger than the paper supplies.

The provider for pushout should therefore be:

    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)

not:

minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief)

Once you make that change, the final branch proof remains short, but the hard proof obligations are exactly the manuscript’s Section 7 obligations rather than vacuous or detached witnesses.

3. §7 central pushout: split into family construction + simple-head determinant

The current packet

lean
minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData

is too compressed. Its fields mix three different manuscript moves:

actual central extension/pushout construction;

quadratic square/polar identities;

the simple-head determinant/radical conclusion from prop:simpleheaddet.

Do not try to prove the whole record in one file. Split it as follows.

New sidecar

File:

lean
import Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockCentralPushoutFinalClosers

noncomputable section
namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

/--
The actual pushout family attached to the prehead central extension.

This should be proved from the literal finite group extension:
`R ≤ K`, `R = Φ(K)`, `K/R ≃ M`, and the pushout by
`λ : R → ZMod 2`.
-/
structure MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutFamilyData {p : FramedPair}
    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    Type where
  phi :
    Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.R → D.M →ₗ[ZMod 2]
      Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.M
  square :
    Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.R → QuadF2 D.M
  square_diagonal :
    ∀ lam m, (square lam).form m = phi lam m m
  polar_symm :
    ∀ lam m m',
      (square lam).polar m m' = phi lam m m' + phi lam m' m
  phi_natural :
    ∀ lam, lam ≠ 0 →
      (∀ c : D.C,
        lam.comp (D.actR c : D.R →ₗ[ZMod 2] D.R) = lam) →
      ∀ c m m',
        phi lam ((D.actM c) m) ((D.actM c) m') = phi lam m m'

/--
The simple-head determinant/radical part of `prop:simpleheaddet`.

This should be proved after `lem:simplehead`, not inside the raw pushout
construction file.
-/
structure MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutSimpleHeadData {p : FramedPair}
    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief}
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutFamilyData D) :
    Type where
  square_annihilates_on_T :
    ∀ lam, lam ≠ 0 →
      (∀ c : D.C,
        lam.comp (D.actR c : D.R →ₗ[ZMod 2] D.R) = lam) →
      Annihilates (F.square lam) D.T
  square_radical_subset_T :
    ∀ lam, lam ≠ 0 →
      (∀ c : D.C,
        lam.comp (D.actR c : D.R →ₗ[ZMod 2] D.R) = lam) →
      ∀ m,
        (∀ m', (F.square lam).polar m m' = 0) →
        m ∈ D.T

def MinimalBlockPreheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData.ofPushoutSplit
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief}
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutFamilyData D)
    (H : MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutSimpleHeadData F) :
    MinimalBlockPreheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData D where
  phi := F.phi
  square := F.square
  square_diagonal := F.square_diagonal
  polar_symm := F.polar_symm
  phi_natural := F.phi_natural
  square_annihilates_on_T := H.square_annihilates_on_T
  square_radical_subset_T := H.square_radical_subset_T

/-
Temporary replacement obligations. These are the manuscript-shaped packets that
should replace the current monolithic residual during the next formalization pass.
-/
axiom minimalBlock_preheadActualPushoutFamilyData
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutFamilyData D)

axiom minimalBlock_preheadActualPushoutSimpleHeadData
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief}
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutFamilyData D) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadActualPushoutSimpleHeadData F)

theorem minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData_from_split
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData D) := by
  obtain ⟨F⟩ := minimalBlock_preheadActualPushoutFamilyData D
  obtain ⟨H⟩ := minimalBlock_preheadActualPushoutSimpleHeadData F
  exact ⟨
    MinimalBlockPreheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData.ofPushoutSplit F H
  ⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

This aligns the Lean proof obligations with the manuscript:

eq:targettower: supplies the actual K, R, K/R, Y/K;

lem:collapse: supplies the Frattini/collapse facts that make R central and exponent two;

lem:simplehead: supplies the simple head M/T;

prop:simpleheaddet: supplies the annihilator/radical determinant statements.

  obtain ⟨H⟩ := minimalBlock_rawSimpleHeadManuscriptData D
  exact ⟨H.noTrivial⟩

theorem minimalBlock_rawHeadSimplicityActualData_from_simpleHead
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockRawTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockRawHeadSimplicityActualData D) := by
  obtain ⟨H⟩ := minimalBlock_rawSimpleHeadManuscriptData D
  exact ⟨H.simplicity⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

A stronger version, if you want to make the manuscript anchor even more explicit, is to include a chosen linear equivalence

lean
D.M ⧸ D.T ≃ₗ[ZMod 2] V

to the actual chief-factor module, together with action compatibility and simplicity of V. Then noTrivial and simplicity become derived fields rather than stored fields. But if the current API does not expose the chief-factor module as a Lean type, the wrapper above is the lowest-risk split.

6. lem:collapse / lower scalar quotient: replace surjectivity by nonzero invariant character

The packet

lean
minimalBlock_rawLowerScalarQuotientData

currently asks for a surjective functional

lean
Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.R

to ZMod 2. Over ZMod 2, surjectivity is just nonzero. The smaller proof obligation should be:

lean
character : Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.R
character_ne_zero : character ≠ 0
character_invariant : ∀ c, character.comp (D.actR c) = character

Then Lean can build the current record.

lean
import Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockProvenanceFinalClosers
import Mathlib.Tactic

noncomputable section
namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

/--
Smaller replacement for `MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData`.

This is the manuscript `lem:collapse` scalar quotient: produce a nonzero
`C`-invariant character of `R`; surjectivity to `ZMod 2` is formal.
-/
structure MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarCharacterData {p : FramedPair}
    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockRawTowerData p chief) :
    Type where
  character :
    Module.Dual (ZMod 2) D.R
  character_ne_zero :
    character ≠ 0
  character_invariant :
    ∀ c : D.C,
      character.comp (D.actR c : D.R →ₗ[ZMod 2] D.R) = character

def MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarCharacterData.toQuotientData
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {D : MinimalBlockRawTowerData p chief}
    (Q : MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarCharacterData D) :
    MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData D where
  quotient := Q.character
  quotient_invariant := Q.character_invariant
  quotient_surjective := by
    intro a
    fin_cases a
    · exact ⟨0, by simp⟩
    · have h_exists : ∃ r : D.R, Q.character r ≠ 0 := by
        by_contra hnone
        apply Q.character_ne_zero
        ext r
        by_contra hr
        exact hnone ⟨r, hr⟩
      obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h_exists
      refine ⟨r, ?_⟩
      have hval : Q.character r = 1 := by
        fin_cases Q.character r <;> simp_all
      simpa [hval]

axiom minimalBlock_rawLowerScalarCharacterData
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockRawTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarCharacterData D)

theorem minimalBlock_rawLowerScalarQuotientData_from_character
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockRawTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockRawLowerScalarQuotientData D) := by
  obtain ⟨Q⟩ := minimalBlock_rawLowerScalarCharacterData D
  exact ⟨Q.toQuotientData⟩

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

This is one of the few places where a residual can be substantially reduced by ordinary Lean proof: only the nonzero invariant character should remain mathematical.

lean
noncomputable section

namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

theorem boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    ¬ ScalarTerminal p → Nonempty (FourierBranchData p) := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨chief⟩ := exists_nonScalarChiefFactor_of_nonScalar p h
  obtain ⟨G⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelModuleSemanticClosers3_noAxiom chief
  obtain ⟨Pdet⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route4Closers_noAxiom
      G.preheadTowerData
  let Fdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData :=
    Pdet.equivariantFamily
  let A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData Fdet :=
    Pdet.annihilationData
  let Rdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData Fdet :=
    Pdet.radicalData
  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet
  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerCurrentIntegrated5
      G h Fdet A Rdet
  exact ⟨fourierBranchData_of B C⟩

theorem boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p) :
    ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
      (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
      framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
      framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated5 p h
  obtain ⟨combine, hA, hQ⟩ :=
    fourier_recursion_step D.W D.dimExp D.zR D.hzR D.children
      D.partialN D.XA D.oA D.XQ D.oQ D.realizeA D.realizeQ
      D.partialA D.partialQ
  exact ⟨D.children, combine, D.small, hA, hQ⟩

theorem boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p ∨
      ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
        (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
        framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
        framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  by_cases h : ScalarTerminal p
  · exact Or.inl (boundaryFramed_scalarTerminal_equal_proven p h)
  · exact Or.inr
      (boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated5 p h)

theorem boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p := by
  suffices H : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ q : FramedPair,
      framedMeasure q = n → framedCountA q = framedCountQ q from
    H (framedMeasure p) p rfl
  intro n
  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with
  | _ n IH =>
    intro q hq
    rcases boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated5 q with hterm |
      ⟨children, combine, hsmall, hA, hQ⟩
    · exact hterm
    · rw [hA, hQ]
      congr 1
      apply List.map_congr_left
      intro c hc
      exact IH (framedMeasure c) (hq ▸ hsmall c hc) c rfl

theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5 :
    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
      Cardinal.mk
        (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) =
      Cardinal.mk
        (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) := by
  intro G _ _ _
  obtain ⟨Frames, Y, hA, hQ⟩ := tameFrameExhaustion_proven G
  rw [hA, hQ]
  congr 1
  funext φ
  exact boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated5 (Y φ)

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers.lean is the main sidecar. It exposes:

lean
MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers
  .minimalBlock_preheadActualCentralExtensionPushoutData_from_route104

MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers
  .minimalBlock_preheadPushoutRouteLowerData_from_route104

MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers
  .minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route104

The key theorem is a drop-in-shaped replacement for the current Route101 pushout provider:

lean
theorem minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route104
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    ∃ F : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData D,
      Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData F) ∧
      Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData F)

IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs104.lean is a no-edit integration route. It imports Route103 and swaps only the pushout/determinant provider inside the nonterminal Fourier branch:

lean
MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers
  .minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route104

The rest of the Route103 candidate/target/kernel plumbing is left intact.

route103_pushout_provider_dropin.patch is the true drop-in option. It modifies:

Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockPushoutRoute101Closers.lean

so that the existing theorem name

lean
MinimalBlockPushoutRoute101Closers
  .minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route101

routes through MinimalBlockExtraspecialClosers3 instead of through MinimalBlockActualCentralExtensionClosers2.

MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104DeepSplitClosers.lean is optional. It routes through MinimalBlockExtraspecialActualClosers4, which appears to split the determinant/pushout floor further into actual scalar comparison and semantic determinant packets. I would treat it as a worker/offload split, not the first promotion candidate.

Exact install commands

From the repository root:

Bash
unzip /path/to/route103_pushout_lowered_artifacts.zip -d [private compute path]
cp -R [private compute path] \
  Q2Presentation/Induction/
chmod +x [private compute path]

Or copy the individual downloaded .lean files directly into:

Q2Presentation/Induction/
Build and audit commands: no-edit Route104 route
Bash
lake build Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockExtraspecialClosers3

lake build Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers

lake build Q2Presentation.Induction.IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs104

Emit provider and integrated-route axiom audits:

Bash
mkdir -p reference/pro_context

lake env lean Q2Presentation/Induction/MinimalBlockPushoutRoute104Closers.lean \
  > reference/pro_context/route104_pushout_provider_axiom_audit.log 2>&1

lake env lean Q2Presentation/Induction/IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs104.lean \
  > reference/pro_context/route104_axiom_audit.log 2>&1

Check that the old five Route103 pushout residuals no longer appear:

Bash
grep -E \
  'minimalBlock_prehead(CentralPushoutSelfDualityData|Extraspecial(NaturalityData|DiagonalZeroData|PolarZeroData|RadicalContainmentData))' \
  reference/pro_context/route104_pushout_provider_axiom_audit.log \
  reference/pro_context/route104_axiom_audit.log

Expected result for successful lowering: no matches.

Then inspect the new lower floor:

Bash
grep -E \
  'minimalBlock_prehead(CentralPushoutSquarePolarData|CentralPushoutSimpleHeadDeterminantActualData|ActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData|CentralPushout(NaturalitySemanticData|DiagonalZeroSemanticData|PolarZeroSemanticData|RadicalContainmentSemanticData))' \
  reference/pro_context/route104_pushout_provider_axiom_audit.log \
  reference/pro_context/route104_axiom_audit.log || true

This audit pattern follows the project’s stated promotion discipline: milestone theorems should be checked through #print axioms, with no hidden local axioms or sorry promoted into the clean route.

centralCover_candidateExactImageResidualData — too broad.

centralCover_targetSubgroupTowerData — currently too weak as provenance.
It names Rsub and Ksub, normality, R ≤ K ≤ L_Y, and 1 < |R|, but the manuscript tower is really R = Φ(K), M = K/R, and T = (K ∩ S)/R; §7 also uses the Frattini–centralizer collapse. Add optional provenance fields or a second structure:

    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)

  Rsub_eq_frattini_Ksub : S.Rsub = frattiniSubgroup S.Ksub
  KmodR_equiv_M : (S.Ksub ⧸ S.Rsub.comap S.Ksub.subtype) ≃+ B.M
  T_realizes_kernel_or_radical : Prop

  Ksub_fourth_power_trivial : Prop

Do not block downstream on this if the current cardinal fields suffice, but use it to guide the eventual full §7 proof.

The field kernelPart plus a cardinal bound is enough for reassembly, but it does not record that kernelPart i is literally J ∩ M. Add a realization field:

or better, define exactImageKernelPart and set kernelPart := exactImageKernelPart.

centralCover_elementaryProperImageExactSequenceData — too cardinal-only.
The manuscript uses the exact sequence 1 → J∩M → J∩L_B → L_C → 1 behind eq:Mdescent; the packet only states the resulting cardinal equality. Add a theorem-level exact sequence witness, then derive the cardinal statement:

Then:

minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialInvariantData — too weak.
Diagonal invariance is enough downstream, but the manuscript’s central-pushout naturality is stronger. Keep the sidecar split:

MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialNaturalityData

and make future workers prove naturality, not merely diagonal invariance.

The sidecar’s DiagonalZeroData + PolarZeroData matches q|T = 0 and b(T,M)=0. Keep that split.

The terminal finite decomposition is already finite group theory; the only genuinely local part is the fully marked pro-2 identification. Long-term split:

The manuscript explicitly requires the full ℤ₂ marking in the terminal compatibility, not just mod-2 data, so do not weaken this interface.

Direct closure strategy

Do not try to prove the current residual packet names verbatim. Several of them are convenience wrappers that quantify over too little coherent provenance. The clean Lean move is:

Reroute candidate §8 through the already-existing semantic exact-image split, not through CandidateRLiftFrattiniClosers.

Replace the §7 scalar square/diagonal/polar packets by projections from one central-pushout semantic family.

Bundle the remaining four determinant semantic packets into one coherent simple-head determinant packet tied to that same family, not four independent axioms.

Split target centralCover_targetKmodRActionCompatibilityData one step further, because its present shape asks for action compatibility from only CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B, while the manuscript proof uses the realized quotient tower Y/R → Y/K and the identification K/R ≃ M.

This is too strong as a free-standing packet over only:

because the manuscript action compatibility is not merely “choose representatives.” It comes from the realized quotient tower

Y/R → Y/K
ker(Y/R → Y/K) ≃ K/R ≃ M

The current CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B knows Rsub, Ksub, Rsub_eq_frattini, and KmodR_equivM, but it does not by itself identify the abstract B.C with the quotient Y/K in the same packet. structure CentralCoverTargetLowerQuotientRealizationData
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B) where
  lowerQuotientEquiv :
    haveI : D.Ksub.Normal := D.Ksub_normal

  quotientRepresentative : B.C → p.1.Y
  quotientRepresentative_spec :
    ∀ c : B.C,

        lowerQuotientEquiv c

    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)

open Q2Presentation.Quadratic

/-!
This sidecar removes the extra "actual scalar central pushout comparison"
residual from the final route.

Manuscript location:
* §7, especially Proposition `simpleheaddet`: after the scalar central
  pushout is available, the associated quadratic form is the one whose
  diagonal and polar are already recorded.
* The comparison packet is therefore not an additional mathematical
  construction: it is an extensionality consequence of the existing
  square-diagonal and polar-symmetry packets.

Expected axiom effect on the final route:
* removes `minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData`;
* keeps the genuine upstream scalar/determinant residuals:

  `minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSquareDiagonalData`,
  `minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutPolarSymmData`,
  and the later semantic determinant packets.
-/

namespace MinimalBlockActualScalarNoComparisonProofs

variable {p : FramedPair}
variable {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
variable {D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief}

/-- The old comparison residual is an extensionality consequence of the
standard square-diagonal and polar-symmetry data. -/
def comparisonDataOfStandard
    (P : MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutStandardData D) :
    MinimalBlockPreheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData P where
  square_is_extraspecial := by
    intro lam
    apply Quadratic.QuadF2.ext
    · funext m
      simpa [Quadratic.Extraspecial.extraspecialForm] using
        P.squareDiagonal.square_diagonal lam m
    · ext m m'
      simpa [Quadratic.Extraspecial.extraspecialForm,
        Quadratic.Extraspecial.polarφ] using
        P.polarSymm.polar_symm lam m m'

/-- Build the actual scalar pushout data directly from the standard packet,
without invoking `minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData`. -/
def actualScalarDataOfStandard
    (P : MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutStandardData D) :
    MinimalBlockPreheadActualScalarCentralPushoutData D where
  phi := P.quadraticFamily.phi
  square := P.quadraticFamily.square
  scalar_quotient_surjective := by
    intro lam hlam
    exact minimalBlock_actualScalar_dual_zmod2_surjective_of_ne_zero lam hlam
  square_is_extraspecial :=
    (comparisonDataOfStandard (D := D) P).square_is_extraspecial

end MinimalBlockActualScalarNoComparisonProofs

/-- Public replacement for the old comparison axiom. -/
theorem minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData_from_standard
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    {D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief}
    (P : MinimalBlockPreheadCentralPushoutStandardData D) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData P) :=
  ⟨MinimalBlockActualScalarNoComparisonProofs.comparisonDataOfStandard (D := D) P⟩

/-- Public scalar-pushout constructor that bypasses the comparison residual. -/
theorem minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutData_from_standard_noComparison
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief) :
    Nonempty (MinimalBlockPreheadActualScalarCentralPushoutData D) := by
  obtain ⟨P⟩ := minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutStandardData_from_packets D
  exact
    ⟨MinimalBlockActualScalarNoComparisonProofs.actualScalarDataOfStandard
      (D := D) P⟩

/-- Replacement semantic pushout route that avoids the comparison residual. -/

patch point for the current nonterminal branch. -/

for the scalar-pushout/determinant call. -/

    (p : FramedPair) :

/-- Recursive branch with the no-comparison scalar-pushout route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
    (p : FramedPair) (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p) :
    ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
      (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
      framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
      framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ :=
    boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
      p h
  obtain ⟨combine, hA, hQ⟩ :=
    fourier_recursion_step D.W D.dimExp D.zR D.hzR D.children
      D.partialN D.XA D.oA D.XQ D.oQ D.realizeA D.realizeQ D.partialA D.partialQ
  exact ⟨D.children, combine, D.small, hA, hQ⟩

/-- Recursion step with the no-comparison scalar-pushout route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_recursion_step_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p ∨
      ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
        (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
        framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
        framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  by_cases h : ScalarTerminal p
  · exact Or.inl (boundaryFramed_scalarTerminal_equal_proven p h)
  · exact Or.inr
      (boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
        p h)

/-- Boundary-framed count equality with the no-comparison scalar-pushout route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_counts_equal_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p := by
  suffices H : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ q : FramedPair,
      framedMeasure q = n → framedCountA q = framedCountQ q from
    H (framedMeasure p) p rfl
  intro n
  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with
  | _ n IH =>
    intro q hq
    rcases
        boundaryFramed_recursion_step_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison
          q with hterm | ⟨children, combine, hsmall, hA, hQ⟩
    · exact hterm
    · rw [hA, hQ]
      congr 1
      apply List.map_congr_left
      intro c hc
      exact IH (framedMeasure c) (hq ▸ hsmall c hc) c rfl

/-- Final finite-surjection equality route whose axiom closure should be the
current canonical route minus
`minimalBlock_preheadActualScalarCentralPushoutComparisonData`. -/
theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison :
    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
      Cardinal.mk
          (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA
            (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) =
        Cardinal.mk
          (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
            (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) := by
  intro G _ _ _
  obtain ⟨Frames, Y, hA, hQ⟩ := tameFrameExhaustion_proven G
  rw [hA, hQ]
  congr 1
  funext φ
  exact
    boundaryFramed_counts_equal_targetFramedMarkedQuotientExtraspecialDeterminantCandidateCanonicalNoComparison

Why this split is strictly narrower

This sidecar does not assert the §7 determinant theorem. It only says: once the existing standard scalar pushout packet has supplied a quadratic family plus its diagonal and polar formulas, the old “comparison” packet follows by QuadF2 extensionality. The remaining determinant residuals correspond to real manuscript content: constructing the family, proving C-naturality, proving vanishing on T, and proving radical containment. Those are the actual §7 propositions about the minimal block and determinant pushout, not mere record-to-record comparison.

Inline patch:

Diff
--- a/Q2Presentation/Induction/CandidateFrattiniRoute54Proofs.lean
+++ b/Q2Presentation/Induction/CandidateFrattiniRoute54Proofs.lean
@@ -41,12 +41,25 @@
       middleObstruction.affineFiberTorsorData.Z
       middleObstruction.affineFiberTorsorData.F
 
-/-- Route54 residual: one coherent exact-image datum instead of seven
-independent scalar/Frattini leaves. -/
-axiom centralCover_candidateRoute54ExactImageData
+/-- Route54 residual closed from the Route53 manuscript-shaped split.
+
+Route53 already supplies the selected middle `R`-obstruction together with the
+matching Frattini exact-image partition.  The Route54 packet asks for the same
+coherent data with the Frattini component in equivalence form, and the
+partition stores exactly such a bijection. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateRoute54ExactImageData
     {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
     (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
-    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute54ExactImageData B T)
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute54ExactImageData B T) := by
+  obtain ⟨D, F⟩ :=
+    centralCover_candidateDirectExactImageResidualData_from_frattiniRoute53
+      B T
+  exact ⟨{

 namespace CentralCoverCandidateRoute54ExactImageData

Full replacement block, in case patch application is inconvenient:

lean
/-- Route54 residual closed from the Route53 manuscript-shaped split.

Route53 already supplies the selected middle `R`-obstruction together with the
matching Frattini exact-image partition.  The Route54 packet asks for the same
coherent data with the Frattini component in equivalence form, and the
partition stores exactly such a bijection. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateRoute54ExactImageData
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute54ExactImageData B T) := by
  obtain ⟨D, F⟩ :=
    centralCover_candidateDirectExactImageResidualData_from_frattiniRoute53
      B T
  exact ⟨{
    middleObstruction := D
    frattiniEquiv := {
      equiv := F.partition.bij
    }
  }⟩

If Lean rejects the F.partition.bij projection due to unfolding/projection elaboration, the equivalent fallback is:

lean
theorem centralCover_candidateRoute54ExactImageData
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute54ExactImageData B T) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ :=
    centralCover_candidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData_from_frattiniRoute53
      B T
  obtain ⟨E⟩ :=
    centralCover_candidateFrattiniAffineFiberCoordinateData_from_frattiniRoute53
      B T D
  exact ⟨{
    middleObstruction := D
    frattiniEquiv := {
      equiv := E.equiv
    }

import Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockDeterminantClosers
import Q2Presentation.Induction.CandidateObstructionFrattiniClosers
import Q2Presentation.Induction.TargetTowerRealizationClosers
import Q2Presentation.Induction.ProperImageExactClosers
import Q2Presentation.Induction.CentralCoverFineResiduals
import Q2Presentation.Induction.FrameExhaustion
import Q2Presentation.Induction.ScalarTerminal
import Q2Presentation.Induction.Recursion

/-!
# Target quotient route through the §7 square/polar determinant packet

This sidecar is intentionally independent of `PresentationCorrect.lean`.

It combines three already-present routes:

* §7 minimal-block construction through
  `minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData_from_squarePolarNaturality`;
* §8 candidate lift partition through
  `centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_obstructionFrattiniClosers`;
* target/proper-image quotient bookkeeping with the middle-kernel equivalence
  carried along with the tower.

The goal is to remove the target subgroup-tower / Frattini / KmodR residual
names from the final `presentation_correct` closure, without regressing to the
older central-pushout self-duality packet or the older candidate quotient-frame
compatibility packet.
-/

noncomputable section

namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

/-- A quotient tower together with the semantic equivalence `ker(B → C) ≃ M`.

`CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData` stores the quotient tower used in the
elementary stage.  Proper-image descent also needs the middle-kernel
identification.  Carrying it here prevents the route from asking for a separate
middle-kernel-equivalence residual for the same tower. -/
structure CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) where
  tower : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B
  middleKernelEquivData : CentralCoverMiddleKernelEquivData B tower

/-- Build the bundled quotient tower from the target packets, retaining the
already-selected middle-kernel identification. -/
def targetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar_of_targetPackets {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) (S : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupTowerData B)
    (Q : CentralCoverTargetQuotientMapsData B S)
    (C : CentralCoverTargetTowerCardinalityData B S Q)
    (K : CentralCoverTargetMiddleKernelIdentificationData B S Q) :
    CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar B where
  tower := elementaryQuotientTowerData_of_targetPackets B S Q C
  middleKernelEquivData := by
    exact { middleKernelEquivM := K.middleKernelEquivM }

/-- The §7 group-tower packet supplies the concrete target subgroup data for the
specific `MinimalBlock` constructed from that same packet. -/
theorem centralCover_targetSubgroupConcreteData_from_selfGroupTowerSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
    (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p)
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData)
    (A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData F)
    (R : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData F) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData (G.toMinimalBlock h F A R)) := by
  exact centralCover_targetSubgroupConcreteData_from_groupTower G
    (G.toMinimalBlock h F A R) G.KmodR_equiv_rawM

/-- Canonical target quotient tower for the minimal block built from the same
§7 group-tower packet.

This route proves the target subgroup tower, Frattini identity, and `K/R ≃ M`
bookkeeping from `G`; the remaining target content is exactly the quotient target
and marked-kernel exactness layer. -/
theorem centralCover_targetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar_from_selfGroupTower
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
    (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p)
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData)
    (A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData F)
    (R : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData F) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar
        (G.toMinimalBlock h F A R)) := by
  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h F A R
  obtain ⟨Dsub⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetSubgroupConcreteData_from_selfGroupTowerSquarePolar
      G h F A R
  let S : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupTowerData B :=
    targetSubgroupTowerData_of_concrete B Dsub
  obtain ⟨DqConcrete⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetQuotientChildConcreteData_from_subgroupConcrete B Dsub
  obtain ⟨Dq⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetQuotientChildEquivData_from_concrete B S ⟨DqConcrete⟩
  obtain ⟨Q⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetQuotientMapsData_from_childEquivs B S ⟨Dq⟩
  obtain ⟨Top⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetTopMarkedExactData_from_subgroupConcrete B Dsub Q
  obtain ⟨Mid⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetMiddleMarkedExactData_from_subgroupConcrete B Dsub Q
  obtain ⟨Ker⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetMiddleKernelIdentificationData_from_subgroupConcrete
      B Dsub Q
  obtain ⟨Exact⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetTowerMarkedExactData_from_parts
      B S Q ⟨Top⟩ ⟨Mid⟩ ⟨Ker⟩
  obtain ⟨Card⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetTowerCardinalityData_from_markedExact B S Q ⟨Exact⟩
  exact
    ⟨targetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar_of_targetPackets
      B S Q Card Ker⟩

/-- Candidate lift-torsor data using a supplied quotient tower, routed through
the newer map-level quotient-frame, R-lift obstruction, and Frattini packets. -/
theorem centralCover_liftTorsorData_fine_integrated_of_quotientTower_obstructionFrattini
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverLiftTorsorData B) := by
  obtain ⟨A⟩ :=
    centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_obstructionFrattiniClosers B T
  obtain ⟨AGamma⟩ :=
    centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData_nonempty B ⟨A⟩
  obtain ⟨AQ⟩ := centralCover_localLiftData_fine B
  exact ⟨liftTorsorData_of_sourceData B AGamma AQ⟩

/-- Elementary-stage children from a supplied canonical quotient tower and its
middle-kernel equivalence. -/
theorem centralCover_elementaryStageChildren_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverElementaryStageChildren B) := by
  obtain ⟨Card⟩ :=
    centralCover_elementaryProperImageCardinalityData_from_exactClosers B D.tower
      ⟨D.middleKernelEquivData⟩
      (centralCover_elementaryProperImageCanonicalRestrictionData B D.tower)
  exact ⟨elementaryStageChildren_of_lowerProper B
    (elementaryLowerTarget_of_quotientTower B D.tower)
    (elementaryProperImageChildrenData_of_family B D.tower
      (elementaryProperImageFamilyData_of_cardinality B D.tower Card))⟩

/-- Central-cover child data from a supplied canonical quotient tower. -/
theorem centralCover_childrenData_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverChildrenData B) := by
  obtain ⟨E⟩ :=
    centralCover_elementaryStageChildren_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar B D
  obtain ⟨P⟩ := centralCover_properImageChildren B
  obtain ⟨Ph⟩ := centralCover_phaseCoverChildren B
  exact ⟨childrenData_of_strata B (childStratum_of_elementaryStage B E) P Ph⟩

/-- Partial recipe from a supplied canonical quotient tower. -/
theorem centralCover_partialRecipe_fine_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar B)
    (L : CentralCoverLiftTorsorData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverPartialRecipe B L) := by
  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
    centralCover_childrenData_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar B D
  obtain ⟨A⟩ := centralCover_candidatePartialData B L C
  obtain ⟨Q⟩ := centralCover_localPartialData B L C
  have h := centralCover_partialRecipeAgreement B L C A Q
  exact ⟨partialRecipe_of_sourceAgreement B L C A Q h⟩

/-- Cover realization from a supplied canonical quotient tower. -/
theorem centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar B) :
    Nonempty (CoverRealization B) := by
  obtain ⟨L⟩ :=
    centralCover_liftTorsorData_fine_integrated_of_quotientTower_obstructionFrattini
      B D.tower
  obtain ⟨R⟩ :=
    centralCover_partialRecipe_fine_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar B D L
  exact ⟨coverRealization_of B L R⟩

/-- Cover realization for the specific minimal block produced by a §7 group
tower, using the square/polar determinant route and the canonical target-quotient
route. -/
theorem centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerTargetSquarePolar
    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
    (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p)
    (F : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData)
    (A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData F)
    (R : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData F) :
    Nonempty (CoverRealization (G.toMinimalBlock h F A R)) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar_from_selfGroupTower
      G h F A R
  exact
    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
      (G.toMinimalBlock h F A R) D

/-- Nonterminal branch routed through the §7 group tower, the square/polar
determinant packet, and the canonical target-quotient route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_targetQuotientSquarePolar
    (p : FramedPair) :
    ¬ ScalarTerminal p → Nonempty (FourierBranchData p) := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨chief⟩ := exists_nonScalarChiefFactor_of_nonScalar p h
  obtain ⟨G⟩ := minimalBlock_groupTowerData chief
  let D : MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData p chief := G.preheadTowerData
  obtain ⟨F⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData_from_squarePolarNaturality D
  obtain ⟨A⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData_from_diagonalPolarResidual F
  obtain ⟨R⟩ :=
    minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialRadicalData_from_containmentResidual F
  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h F A R
  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerTargetSquarePolar
      G h F A R
  exact ⟨fourierBranchData_of B C⟩

/-- Recursive branch using the canonical target-quotient / square-polar route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_targetQuotientSquarePolar
    (p : FramedPair) (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p) :
    ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
      (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
      framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
      framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ :=
    boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_targetQuotientSquarePolar p h
  obtain ⟨combine, hA, hQ⟩ :=
    fourier_recursion_step D.W D.dimExp D.zR D.hzR D.children
      D.partialN D.XA D.oA D.XQ D.oQ D.realizeA D.realizeQ
      D.partialA D.partialQ
  exact ⟨D.children, combine, D.small, hA, hQ⟩

/-- Recursion step using the canonical target-quotient / square-polar branch. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_recursion_step_targetQuotientSquarePolar
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p ∨
      ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
        (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
        framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
        framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
  by_cases h : ScalarTerminal p
  · exact Or.inl (boundaryFramed_scalarTerminal_equal_proven p h)
  · exact Or.inr
      (boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_targetQuotientSquarePolar p h)

/-- Boundary-framed counts equal through the canonical target-quotient /
square-polar route. -/
theorem boundaryFramed_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar
    (p : FramedPair) :
    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p := by
  suffices H : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ q : FramedPair,
      framedMeasure q = n → framedCountA q = framedCountQ q from
    H (framedMeasure p) p rfl
  intro n
  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with
  | _ n IH =>
    intro q hq
    rcases boundaryFramed_recursion_step_targetQuotientSquarePolar q with hterm |
      ⟨children, combine, hsmall, hA, hQ⟩
    · exact hterm
    · rw [hA, hQ]
      congr 1
      apply List.map_congr_left
      intro c hc
      exact IH (framedMeasure c) (hq ▸ hsmall c hc) c rfl

/-- Equal finite surjection counts through the canonical target-quotient /
square-polar route. -/
theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar :
    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
      Cardinal.mk
          (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA
            (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G)))
        =
        Cardinal.mk
          (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
            (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) := by
  intro G _ _ _
  obtain ⟨Frames, Y, hA, hQ⟩ := tameFrameExhaustion_proven G
  rw [hA, hQ]
  congr 1
  funext φ
  exact boundaryFramed_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar (Y φ)

section Acceptance

#print axioms centralCover_targetSubgroupConcreteData_from_selfGroupTowerSquarePolar
#print axioms centralCover_targetCanonicalQuotientTowerDataSquarePolar_from_selfGroupTower
#print axioms centralCover_liftTorsorData_fine_integrated_of_quotientTower_obstructionFrattini
#print axioms centralCover_elementaryStageChildren_from_canonicalQuotientTowerSquarePolar
#print axioms centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerTargetSquarePolar
#print axioms finite_surjection_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar

end Acceptance

end Induction

open Profinite Induction

/-- Surjection-count equality packaged for the reconstruction lemma, using the
canonical target-quotient / square-polar route. -/
theorem surj_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar
    (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] :
    Cardinal.mk
        (SurjContHom GammaA
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G)))
      =
      Cardinal.mk
        (SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
          (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) :=
  haveI : Nonempty G := ⟨1⟩
  finite_surjection_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar G

section Acceptance

#print axioms surj_counts_equal_targetQuotientSquarePolar

end Acceptance

end Q2Presentation

  middleToLower_maps_LB :

    (B : MinimalBlock p) (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) where
  restricted_surj :
    ∀ i : CentralCoverElementaryProperExactImageSubgroups B T,
      ∀ y : T.lowerChild.1.LY,
        ∃ x : T.middleChild.1.LY.subgroupOf i.1,
          T.middleToLower (elementaryProperImageRestrictedDomainVal x) = y

def elementaryProperImageCanonicalRestrictionData_of_markedRestriction
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B)
    (M : CentralCoverMiddleToLowerMarkedRestrictionData B T)

  middleKernel_le_marked := M.middleKernel_le_marked
  mapsToLowerMarked := by

The first structure is quotient-map exactness. The second is the exact-image/proper-image descent statement. Keeping them separate should make the next proof search much more honest.
