Based on the uploaded repository snapshot, my audit verdict is:

The sidecar is a good field-level decomposition of the two coarse residuals, but I would not accept it as a fully faithful main-clean refinement yet. The lift-torsor split is structurally faithful. The partial-count split has two serious underconstraint problems: centralCover_childrenData is trivially closeable by children := [], and centralCover_partialSkeleton is trivially closeable by an arbitrary function. Worse, the candidate/local partial-realization axioms are stated for every skeleton N, which is too strong and can make the residual package inconsistent once arbitrary skeletons are constructed.

I treated the uploaded files as the source of truth; I could not independently run lake env lean in this sandbox, so the axiom-closure comments below are a static dependency audit assuming the file really builds.

1. Faithful refinement, or vacuous shortcut?

Not fully. More precisely:

The lift refinement is faithful at the current interface level. CentralCoverSourceLiftData contains exactly one source’s finite lift index X, obstruction map o, cocycle group Z, cardinality proof Nat.card Z = B.zR, lift torsors, and a bijection from the relevant boundaryFramedSurj set to unobstructed lift fibres. The candidate and local axioms are then combined field-by-field into CentralCoverLiftTorsorData by liftTorsorData_of_sourceData (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:48-116). This really refines the older two-source residual rather than calling centralCover_liftTorsorData.

It is not a shortcut through an empty or one-point MinimalBlock. The current MinimalBlock is not just a dummy handle: it has nonScalar, a nontrivial simple head, a nonzero invariant character, dual-invariant vanishing, and pushout annihilation/radical/nonzero/invariance fields (MinimalBlock.lean:228-299). So the sidecar is not manufacturing a block from an underconstrained MinimalBlock.

However, the partial-count refinement is underconstrained:

lean
structure CentralCoverChildrenData {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p) where
  children : List FramedPair
  small : forall c, c ∈ children -> framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p

This admits the empty list, so it does not by itself encode “the targets occurring on the right sides” of the manuscript’s recursive formulas (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:123-129). The manuscript’s lem:strictdecrease is not merely “there exists some decreasing list”; it says every target produced by the elementary-stage, proper-image, scalar-cover, and phase-cover transforms has smaller marked 2-kernel (q2_manuscript(4).tex:4399-4425).

Likewise,

lean
structure CentralCoverPartialSkeleton ... where
  partialN : Module.Dual (ZMod 2) B.obstructionSpace -> List Nat -> Nat

contains no formula, no proper-image subtraction data, no edge case data, no phase-cover data, and no relation to the children beyond receiving a List Nat later (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:137-144). So it is only a placeholder for the shared recipe, not a faithful formal encoding of the recipe assembled in lem:properimagesubtraction, prop:nonzeroedge, prop:zeroedge, prop:phaseidentity, and lem:phasecover.

The most serious issue is the quantification of the source partial-realization residuals:

lean
axiom centralCover_candidatePartialRealization ... (N : CentralCoverPartialSkeleton B T C) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidatePartialRealization B T C N)

and similarly for the local source (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:166-179). Since N is universally quantified, the axiom says the same actual count equals any supplied function N.partialN. But skeletons with different partialN functions are easy to construct. That is not just “broad”; it is mathematically unsafe.

A proof-sketch of the problem:

lean
let C : CentralCoverChildrenData B :=
  { children := [], small := by intro c hc; cases hc }

let N0 : CentralCoverPartialSkeleton B T C :=
  { partialN := fun _ _ => 0 }

let N1 : CentralCoverPartialSkeleton B T C :=
  { partialN := fun _ _ => 1 }

obtain ⟨A0⟩ := centralCover_candidatePartialRealization B T C N0
obtain ⟨A1⟩ := centralCover_candidatePartialRealization B T C N1

have h0 := A0.partialA 0
have h1 := A1.partialA 0
-- same left-hand Nat.card equals 0 and 1

That shows the current partial-realization residuals are not faithful as stated. The issue is not the old centralCover_realization axiom, and not MinimalBlock; it is the new skeleton/realization interface.

2. Are the six finer residual axioms materially smaller and manuscript-anchored?

The two lift residuals: yes.
centralCover_candidateLiftData and centralCover_localLiftData are materially smaller than centralCover_liftTorsorData: each handles one source only, and each carries only the lift-torsor/orbit data needed for that source. They are plausibly anchored to def:Xgamma, lem:covertransform, lem:affinelifting, and prop:finalfourier, whose manuscript roles are exact-image lower maps, central-cover exact-image partition, affine lifting, and final Fourier inversion (q2_manuscript(4).tex:3784-3789, 3817-3857, 4048-4083, 4240-4265).

centralCover_childrenData: smaller, but too weak.
It isolates strict decrease, but because children := [] satisfies it, it does not materially capture the children created by the actual Section 8 transforms. It is manuscript-named, but not manuscript-constraining.

centralCover_partialSkeleton: smaller, but not materially anchored in its current form.
It names the shared source-independent recipe, but stores only an arbitrary function. The manuscript’s recipe is a structured expression assembled from proper-image subtraction, nonzero/zero radical-edge formulas, phase identity, and phase-cover transforms (q2_manuscript(4).tex:4267-4397). The Lean structure does not encode those constituents.

centralCover_candidatePartialRealization and centralCover_localPartialRealization: smaller, but over-quantified.
Each is source-specific, which is a real refinement. But because each must realize every N, the statement is stronger than the manuscript and likely inconsistent with arbitrary skeletons. These should realize the selected manuscript skeleton, not every skeleton.

So the six-way split is directionally good, but only four of the six are meaningful as mathematical residuals in their current shape, and the two partial-realization axioms are currently too strong.

3. Is any residual closeable from the current Lean library without an equivalent axiom?

Yes, but only for bad reasons.

The easiest closeable residual is centralCover_childrenData, because the structure accepts an empty list:

lean
theorem centralCover_childrenData_trivial {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverChildrenData B) := by
  exact ⟨{
    children := []
    small := by
      intro c hc
      cases hc
  }⟩

This uses no Section 8 mathematics. That is a red flag: it proves the current residual does not faithfully express lem:strictdecrease.

The second trivially closeable residual is centralCover_partialSkeleton:

lean
theorem centralCover_partialSkeleton_trivial {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) (T : CentralCoverLiftTorsorData B)
    (C : CentralCoverChildrenData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverPartialSkeleton B T C) := by
  exact ⟨{
    partialN := fun _ _ => 0
  }⟩

Again, this is not a manuscript proof; it shows the skeleton is currently only a placeholder.

For the four meaningful sourcewise residuals, I do not see a plausible close from the current Lean library without essentially introducing the missing Section 8 machinery as an equivalent axiom:

centralCover_candidateLiftData and centralCover_localLiftData need actual exact-image lower maps, central-cover orbit partitioning, obstruction maps, affine lift torsors, and the source-specific bijections. That is exactly the manuscript’s def:Xgamma, lem:covertransform, lem:affinelifting, and the lift half of prop:finalfourier.

centralCover_candidatePartialRealization and centralCover_localPartialRealization, once corrected to refer to a selected shared skeleton, need proper-image subtraction, nonzero/zero edge formulas, completed-square phase identity, and phase-cover transform. These are not generic mathlib lemmas; they are the project’s finite exact-image/Fourier recursion content. The plan’s main-clean discipline also requires no custom mathematical axioms or hidden sorry in accepted theorems, so simply replacing these with opaque constants would not satisfy the project discipline. 

plan_4turns

4. Are the expected axiom closures correct?

Mostly yes, with one important caveat: the closures will be structurally correct, but the residual set they expose is not yet safe because of the partial-skeleton issue.

For centralCover_liftTorsorData_refined, the expected closure is:

standard axioms
+ upstream boundary-package axioms appearing through boundaryFramedSurj / boundaryPackage_GammaA / boundaryPackage_GQ2
+ centralCover_candidateLiftData
+ centralCover_localLiftData

and not centralCover_liftTorsorData or centralCover_realization. The proof directly obtains the two source packets and reassembles them (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:112-116). This closure expectation is correct.

For centralCover_partialRecipe_refined, the expected closure is:

standard axioms
+ upstream boundary-package axioms carried by CentralCoverLiftTorsorData’s type
+ centralCover_childrenData
+ centralCover_partialSkeleton
+ centralCover_candidatePartialRealization
+ centralCover_localPartialRealization

and not centralCover_partialRecipe. The proof obtains those four finer residuals and reassembles CentralCoverPartialRecipe field-by-field (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:197-204). This closure expectation is correct.

For centralCover_realization_proven_refined, the expected closure is:

standard axioms
+ upstream boundary-package/local package axioms
+ centralCover_candidateLiftData
+ centralCover_localLiftData
+ centralCover_childrenData
+ centralCover_partialSkeleton
+ centralCover_candidatePartialRealization
+ centralCover_localPartialRealization

and not:

centralCover_realization
centralCover_liftTorsorData
centralCover_partialRecipe

The proof goes through centralCover_liftTorsorData_refined, centralCover_partialRecipe_refined, and then coverRealization_of (CentralCoverResidualRefinement.lean:214-218). Since coverRealization_of is a definition reassembling fields, not the old axiom, the old three residuals should not appear.

The upstream boundary-package closure should include whatever #print axioms boundaryPackage_GammaA and #print axioms boundaryPackage_GQ2 report. The uploaded aggregate file documents boundaryPackage_GQ2 as depending on the local marked Labute axiom and the local tame quotient axiom, namely labute_GQ2_maxPro2_marked and gq2_tame_quotient (project_so_far(3).lean:57-68, 168-199). Candidate-side boundary package dependencies appear to be the candidate tame/pro-2 package theorems rather than new Section 8 residuals (project_so_far(3).lean:287-301).

Acceptance recommendation

I would not accept the sidecar as main-clean in its current form. It succeeds at exposing finer names and avoiding the old coarse residuals, but it introduces a new underconstraint/overconstraint problem in the partial-count layer.

The blocking issue is this: CentralCoverPartialSkeleton should not be arbitrary, and the candidate/local partial-realization residuals should not be universally quantified over every skeleton. As written, the trivial skeletons make the residual package too weak in one place and too strong in another. The lift-torsor half is acceptable as a refinement; the partial-count half needs tightening before the #print axioms closure is meaningful.

+    TargetKmodRRoute76BridgeClosers.CentralCoverTargetRoute76AmbientKmodRActionData
+      B D (route76ConcreteLowerProjectionData_of_route77 B D M S K) where
+  ambient_action_realizes_conjugation := by
+    intro y m
+    let P : CentralCoverTargetLowerProjectionData B D :=
+      lowerProjectionData_of_route77 B D M S K
+    let R :
+        TargetKmodRSameTowerBridgeClosers.CentralCoverTargetKmodRRepresentativeActionBridgeData
+          B D P :=
+      representativeBridgeData_of_route77 B D M S K A
+    obtain ⟨I⟩ := centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData_from_KmodR_bridge B D
+    have h :=
+      (TargetKmodRSameTowerBridgeClosers.projectedConjugationActionData_of_representativeBridge
+        B D P R I).action_realizes_projected_conjugation y m
+    simpa [P, lowerProjectionData_of_route77,
+      route76ConcreteLowerProjectionData_of_route77] using h
+
+theorem centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_route77 :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverTargetDecorationKernelData B D) := by
+  obtain ⟨Aquot⟩ := centralCover_targetKmodRQuotientActionData_from_route77 B D
+  let Acompat :
+      TargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceProofs.CentralCoverTargetKmodRActionCompatibilityData
+        B D :=
+    TargetKmodRActionProofs.kmodRActionCompatibilityData_of_quotientAction
+      B D Aquot
+  let Hinvar :
+      TargetDecorationActualDescentProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceData
+        B D :=
+    TargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceProofs.canonicalInvarianceData_of_kmodRActionCompatibility
+      B D Acompat
+  let Hactual :
+      TargetDecorationBlockMapActualProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationActualDescentData
+        B D :=
+    TargetDecorationActualDescentProofs.actualDescentData_of_canonicalInvariance
+      B D Hinvar
+  let Hblock :
+      TargetDecorationKernelProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationBlockMapData B D :=
+    TargetDecorationBlockMapActualProofs.blockMapData_of_actualDescent
+      B D Hactual
+  exact

+      B D Hblock⟩

+import Q2Presentation.Induction.CandidateScalarFrattiniActualClosers

+/-!
+# Candidate scalar/Frattini Route74 closers
+
+Route74 is a candidate-side sidecar for the Route73 work frontier.  It keeps the
+same public provider shape as Route72
+
+```
+B T Q -> Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B)
+```
+
+but replaces the seven independent Route73 candidate atoms by one coherent
+selected scalar/Frattini packet.
+
+The selected packet contains the actual middle `R`-valued obstruction map and a
+matched Frattini coordinate system for that same obstruction.  Pointwise scalar
+pushout values and scalar additivity/homogeneity are then theorem-level
+projections from the linear-map structure of the selected `R`-valued obstruction,
+rather than independent atoms.  The Frattini base/fibre/realization/inverse data
+are projections from one coherent coordinate system, rather than independent
+packets for arbitrary externally supplied choices.
+-/
+
+noncomputable section
+
+namespace Q2Presentation
+namespace Induction
+
+/-! ## Scalar pushout projections from a selected middle obstruction -/
+
+namespace CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData
+
+variable {p : FramedPair} {B : MinimalBlock p}
+  {T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B}
+
+/-- Route74 pointwise scalar-pushout value obtained by evaluating the selected
+`R`-valued middle obstruction against `lambda : X_R`. -/
+def route74ScalarPushoutValueAtData
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T)
+    (lam : ↥B.XR) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutValueAtData B T lam where
+  valueAt := fun x => D.obstruction x lam
+
+/-- Route74 scalar-pushout family induced by the selected middle obstruction. -/
+def route74ScalarPushoutFamilyData
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutFamilyData B T where
+  valueAt := fun lam => D.route74ScalarPushoutValueAtData lam
+
+/-- Additivity in `lambda` follows from linearity of the obstruction value in
+`B.obstructionSpace = Module.Dual (ZMod 2) B.XR`. -/
+def route74ScalarPushoutAdditivityData
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutAdditivityData
+      D.route74ScalarPushoutFamilyData where
+  map_add := fun x lam mu => (D.obstruction x).map_add lam mu
+
+/-- Homogeneity in `lambda` follows from linearity of the obstruction value. -/
+def route74ScalarPushoutHomogeneityData
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutHomogeneityData
+      D.route74ScalarPushoutFamilyData where
+  map_smul := fun x a lam => (D.obstruction x).map_smul a lam
+
+/-- Scalar-obstruction evaluator reassembled from the selected scalar-pushout
+family. -/
+def route74ScalarObstructionEvaluatorData
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarObstructionEvaluatorData B T :=
+  D.route74ScalarPushoutFamilyData.evaluatorData
+    D.route74ScalarPushoutAdditivityData
+    D.route74ScalarPushoutHomogeneityData
+
+end CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData
+
+/-! ## Coherent selected Frattini coordinate system -/
+
+/-- Route74 selected Frattini coordinate system for a fixed middle obstruction.
+
+This is deliberately weaker than the five Route73 Frattini atoms: it asks only
+for one coherent base coordinate, fibre coordinate, realization, and inverse-law
+pair for the selected obstruction used in the route, rather than independent
+base/fibre/realization packets and inverse laws for arbitrary externally supplied
+coordinates. -/
+structure CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B)
+    (D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) : Type where
+  base : CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniBaseCoordinateData B T D
+  fiber : CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniFiberCoordinateData base
+  realization : CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniFiberRealizationData B T D
+  realize_lift :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniRealizeLiftCoordinateData base fiber realization
+  lift_realize :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniLiftCoordinateRealizeData base fiber realization
+
+namespace CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData
+
+variable {p : FramedPair} {B : MinimalBlock p}
+  {T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B}
+  {D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T}
+
+/-- Reassemble the older lift-coordinate packet from the selected coordinate
+system. -/
+def liftCoordinateData
+    (S : CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T D) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniLiftCoordinateData B T D :=
+  S.base.liftCoordinateData S.fiber
+
+/-- Reassemble the older realization packet from the selected coordinate system. -/
+def realizationData
+    (S : CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T D) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniRealizationData B T D :=
+  S.realization.realizationData
+
+/-- Reassemble the older inverse-law packet from the selected coordinate system. -/
+def coordinateInverseData
+    (S : CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T D) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniCoordinateInverseData
+      S.liftCoordinateData S.realizationData where
+  realize_liftCoordinate := S.realize_lift.realize_liftCoordinate
+  liftCoordinate_realize := S.lift_realize.liftCoordinate_realize
+
+/-- Reassemble the current Frattini affine-fibre coordinate packet. -/
+def affineFiberCoordinateData
+    (S : CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T D) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniAffineFiberCoordinateData B T D :=
+  S.liftCoordinateData.coordinateData S.realizationData S.coordinateInverseData
+
+/-- Reassemble the exact-image partition from the selected coordinate system. -/
+def frattiniExactImage
+    (S : CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T D) :
+    CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniExactImageSurjectivityData B T
+      D.obstructionIndexToBaseMapData.O
+      D.affineFiberTorsorData.Z
+      D.affineFiberTorsorData.F where
+  partition := {
+    bij := S.affineFiberCoordinateData.equiv
+  }
+
+end CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData
+
+/-! ## One selected scalar/Frattini packet -/
+
+/-- Route74's single candidate-side residual.
+
+It packages the selected middle `R`-valued obstruction map together with the
+matching selected Frattini coordinate system for that same obstruction.  This is
+the manuscript-shaped data needed by `lem:obstructionseparation`,
+`lem:affinelifting`, and `prop:finalfourier` at the current API level. -/
+structure CentralCoverCandidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) : Type where
+  middleObstruction : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T
+  frattiniSystem :
+    CentralCoverCandidateRoute74FrattiniCoordinateSystemData B T middleObstruction
+
+/-- The one remaining Route74 candidate atom: one coherent selected middle
+obstruction and Frattini coordinate system. -/
+axiom centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData B T)
+
+/-- Public wrapper for the selected Route74 scalar/Frattini packet. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData B T) :=
+  centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData B T
+
+/-! ## Projections closing the Route73 atoms at the selected route call site -/
+
+/-- Pointwise scalar-pushout value, projected from the selected middle
+obstruction. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarPushoutValueAtData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) (lam : ↥B.XR) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutValueAtData B T lam) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨S.middleObstruction.route74ScalarPushoutValueAtData lam⟩
+
+/-- Coherent selected scalar family with both linearity laws, projected from the
+same selected middle obstruction. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarPushoutLinearityData
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty
+      (Sigma fun V : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutFamilyData B T =>
+        CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutAdditivityData V ×
+          CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarPushoutHomogeneityData V) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact
+    ⟨S.middleObstruction.route74ScalarPushoutFamilyData,
+      S.middleObstruction.route74ScalarPushoutAdditivityData,
+      S.middleObstruction.route74ScalarPushoutHomogeneityData⟩
+
+/-- Scalar-obstruction evaluator from the selected middle obstruction. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarObstructionEvaluatorData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateMiddleScalarObstructionEvaluatorData B T) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨S.middleObstruction.route74ScalarObstructionEvaluatorData⟩
+
+/-- Middle obstruction-map packet from the selected Route74 data. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨S.middleObstruction⟩
+
+/-- Frattini affine-fibre coordinate packet for the selected obstruction. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateFrattiniAffineFiberCoordinateData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty
+      (Sigma fun D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T =>
+        CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniAffineFiberCoordinateData B T D) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨S.middleObstruction, S.frattiniSystem.affineFiberCoordinateData⟩
+
+/-- Frattini exact-image partition for the selected obstruction. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateFrattiniAffineFiberPartition_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty
+      (Sigma fun D : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData B T =>
+        CentralCoverCandidateFrattiniExactImageSurjectivityData B T
+          D.obstructionIndexToBaseMapData.O
+          D.affineFiberTorsorData.Z
+          D.affineFiberTorsorData.F) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨S.middleObstruction, S.frattiniSystem.frattiniExactImage⟩
+
+/-! ## Route-ready reassembly -/
+
+/-- Coherent Route74 direct exact-image residual using one selected scalar/Frattini
+packet. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateDirectExactImageResidualData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateDirectExactImageResidualData B T) := by
+  obtain ⟨S⟩ := centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattini B T
+  exact ⟨{
+    middleObstruction := S.middleObstruction
+    frattiniPartition := S.frattiniSystem.frattiniExactImage
+  }⟩
+
+/-- Drop-in Route74 candidate provider for the integrated route.
+
+The axiom closure of this theorem should contain the single selected
+scalar/Frattini atom above, and not the seven Route73 scalar/Frattini atoms. -/
+theorem centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_route74
+    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
+    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B)
+    (Q : CentralCoverCandidateQuotientFrameMapData B T) :
+    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) := by
+  obtain ⟨D⟩ := centralCover_candidateDirectExactImageResidualData_from_route74 B T
+  exact ⟨D.liftPartitionData Q⟩
+
+section Acceptance
+
+#print axioms centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarPushoutValueAtData_from_route74
+#print axioms centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarPushoutLinearityData
+#print axioms centralCover_candidateMiddleScalarObstructionEvaluatorData_from_route74
+#print axioms centralCover_candidateMiddleRLiftObstructionMapData_from_route74

+#print axioms centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_route74
+
+end Acceptance
+
+end Induction
+end Q2Presentation

+import Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockPushoutRoute72Closers
+import Q2Presentation.Induction.TargetKmodRRoute72ProjectionActionClosers
+import Q2Presentation.Induction.MinimalBlockKernelRoute72FrattiniClosers
+import Q2Presentation.Induction.CandidateScalarFrattiniRoute74Closers
+
+/-!
+# Integrated current route, seventy-fourth refinement
+
+Route74 preserves Route73's target, kernel, and determinant/pushout providers,
+and replaces only the candidate exact-image/Frattini provider:
+
+* candidate exact-image/Frattini lifting is supplied by
+  `centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_route74`, replacing the seven
+  Route73 scalar/Frattini coordinate atoms with the single coherent selected
+  packet `centralCover_candidateRoute74SelectedScalarFrattiniData`;
+* minimal-block least/Frattini tower data is still supplied by
+  `minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelRoute72FrattiniClosers`;
+* target `K/R` decoration is still supplied by
+  `TargetKmodRRoute72ProjectionActionClosers.centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_route72`;
+* the minimal-block determinant/pushout provider is still supplied by
+  `minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route72`.
+
+This file intentionally leaves earlier route files untouched and exposes the
+current theorem under `currentIntegrated74` names for candidate-side audit.
+-/
+
+noncomputable section
+
+namespace Q2Presentation
+namespace Induction
+
+theorem centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerCurrentIntegrated74
+    {p : FramedPair} {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
+    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerData p chief)
+    (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p)
+    (Fdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialEquivariantFamilyData G.preheadTowerData)
+    (A : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialAnnihilationData Fdet)
+    (Rdet : MinimalBlockPreheadExtraspecialRadicalData Fdet) :
+    Nonempty (CoverRealization (G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet)) := by
+  let B : MinimalBlock p := G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet
+  obtain ⟨Dsub⟩ :=
+    centralCover_targetSubgroupConcreteData_from_selfGroupTower G h Fdet A Rdet
+  obtain ⟨Hker⟩ :=
+    TargetKmodRRoute72ProjectionActionClosers.centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_route72
+      B Dsub
+  let Fframed : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalFramedQuotientData B Dsub :=
+    TargetCanonicalFramedQuotientProofs.framedQuotientDataOfDecorationKernel B Dsub Hker
+  let F : CentralCoverTargetFramedMarkedQuotientData B Dsub := {
+    framed := Fframed
+    topMarked := targetTopMarkedRestrictionSurjData_of_framedQuotient B Dsub Fframed
+    middleMarked := targetMiddleMarkedRestrictionSurjData_of_framedQuotient B Dsub Fframed
+    middleAmbientKernelMarked :=
+      targetMiddleAmbientKernelMarkedData_of_framedQuotient B Dsub Fframed
+  }
+  let S : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupTowerData B :=
+    targetSubgroupTowerData_of_concrete B Dsub
+  let Dq : CentralCoverTargetQuotientChildEquivData B S :=
+    targetQuotientChildEquivData_of_framedQuotient B Dsub F.framed
+  let Q : CentralCoverTargetQuotientMapsData B S :=
+    targetQuotientMapsData_of_childEquivs B S Dq
+  let Top : CentralCoverTargetTopMarkedExactData B S Q :=
+    targetTopMarkedExactData_of_framedPacket B Dsub F
+  let Dmid : CentralCoverTargetMiddleMarkedRestrictionData B S Q :=
+    targetMiddleMarkedRestrictionData_of_framedPacket B Dsub F
+  let Kmid : CentralCoverTargetMiddleMarkedKernelOfRestrictionData B S Q Dmid :=
+    targetMiddleMarkedKernelOfRestrictionData_of_framedPacket B Dsub F
+  let Mid : CentralCoverTargetMiddleMarkedExactData B S Q :=
+    targetMiddleMarkedExactData_of_mapKernel B S Q
+      (targetMiddleMarkedMapData_of_restriction B S Q Dmid)
+      (targetMiddleMarkedKernelData_of_restriction B S Q Dmid Kmid)
+  obtain ⟨Ker⟩ :=
+    centralCover_targetMiddleKernelIdentificationData_from_childEquivs_actualClosers
+      B Dsub Dq
+  obtain ⟨Exact⟩ :=
+    centralCover_targetTowerMarkedExactData_from_parts B S Q ⟨Top⟩ ⟨Mid⟩ ⟨Ker⟩
+  obtain ⟨Card⟩ := centralCover_targetTowerCardinalityData_from_markedExact B S Q ⟨Exact⟩
+  let T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B :=
+    elementaryQuotientTowerData_of_targetPackets B S Q Card
+  let Dcan : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerData B :=
+    targetCanonicalQuotientTowerData_of_targetPackets B S Q Card Ker
+  obtain ⟨PI⟩ :=
+    centralCover_elementaryProperImageCardinalityData_from_exactClosers B T
+      ⟨Dcan.middleKernelEquivData⟩
+      (centralCover_elementaryProperImageCanonicalRestrictionData_from_targetMiddleRestriction
+        B S Q Card Dmid Kmid)
+  let E : CentralCoverElementaryStageChildren B :=
+    elementaryStageChildren_of_lowerProper B
+      (elementaryLowerTarget_of_quotientTower B T)
+      (elementaryProperImageChildrenData_of_family B T
+        (elementaryProperImageFamilyData_of_cardinality B T PI))
+  obtain ⟨Pchildren⟩ := centralCover_properImageChildren B
+  obtain ⟨Phchildren⟩ := centralCover_phaseCoverChildren B
+  let Cchildren : CentralCoverChildrenData B :=
+    childrenData_of_strata B (childStratum_of_elementaryStage B E)
+      Pchildren Phchildren
+  let Qframe : CentralCoverCandidateQuotientFrameMapData B T :=
+    centralCover_candidateQuotientFrameMapData_of_decorationKernel B Dsub Hker Card
+  obtain ⟨AΓ⟩ :=
+    centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionData_from_route74 B T Qframe
+  obtain ⟨LΓ⟩ := centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData_nonempty B ⟨AΓ⟩
+  obtain ⟨LQ⟩ := centralCover_localLiftData_fine B
+  let L : CentralCoverLiftTorsorData B :=
+    liftTorsorData_of_sourceData B LΓ LQ
+  obtain ⟨PartA⟩ := centralCover_candidatePartialData B L Cchildren
+  obtain ⟨PartQ⟩ := centralCover_localPartialData B L Cchildren
+  have hAgree := centralCover_partialRecipeAgreement B L Cchildren PartA PartQ
+  let Recipe : CentralCoverPartialRecipe B L :=
+    partialRecipe_of_sourceAgreement B L Cchildren PartA PartQ hAgree
+  exact ⟨coverRealization_of B L Recipe⟩
+
+theorem boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated74
+    (p : FramedPair) :
+    ¬ ScalarTerminal p → Nonempty (FourierBranchData p) := by
+  intro h
+  obtain ⟨chief⟩ := exists_nonScalarChiefFactor_of_nonScalar p h
+  obtain ⟨G⟩ := minimalBlock_groupTowerData_from_kernelRoute72FrattiniClosers chief
+  obtain ⟨Fdet, hA, hR⟩ :=
+    minimalBlock_preheadDeterminantPackets_from_route72 G.preheadTowerData
+  obtain ⟨A⟩ := hA
+  obtain ⟨Rdet⟩ := hR
+  obtain ⟨C⟩ :=
+    centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerCurrentIntegrated74
+      G h Fdet A Rdet
+  exact ⟨fourierBranchData_of (G.toMinimalBlock h Fdet A Rdet) C⟩
+
+theorem boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated74
+    (p : FramedPair) (h : ¬ ScalarTerminal p) :
+    ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
+      (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
+      framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
+      framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
+  obtain ⟨D⟩ := boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated74 p h
+  obtain ⟨combine, hA, hQ⟩ :=
+    fourier_recursion_step D.W D.dimExp D.zR D.hzR D.children
+      D.partialN D.XA D.oA D.XQ D.oQ D.realizeA D.realizeQ D.partialA D.partialQ
+  exact ⟨D.children, combine, D.small, hA, hQ⟩
+
+theorem boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated74
+    (p : FramedPair) :
+    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p ∨
+      ∃ (children : List FramedPair) (combine : List Cardinal → Cardinal),
+        (∀ c ∈ children, framedMeasure c < framedMeasure p) ∧
+        framedCountA p = combine (children.map framedCountA) ∧
+        framedCountQ p = combine (children.map framedCountQ) := by
+  by_cases h : ScalarTerminal p
+  · exact Or.inl (boundaryFramed_scalarTerminal_equal_proven p h)
+  · exact Or.inr
+      (boundaryFramed_recursive_branch_from_fourier_currentIntegrated74 p h)
+
+theorem boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated74
+    (p : FramedPair) :
+    framedCountA p = framedCountQ p := by
+  suffices H : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ q : FramedPair,
+      framedMeasure q = n → framedCountA q = framedCountQ q from
+    H (framedMeasure p) p rfl
+  intro n
+  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with
+  | _ n IH =>
+    intro q hq
+    rcases boundaryFramed_recursion_step_currentIntegrated74 q with hterm |
+      ⟨children, combine, hsmall, hA, hQ⟩
+    · exact hterm
+    · rw [hA, hQ]
+      congr 1
+      apply List.map_congr_left
+      intro c hc
+      exact IH (framedMeasure c) (hq ▸ hsmall c hc) c rfl
+
+theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated74 :
+    ∀ (G : Type) [Group G] [Finite G] [Nonempty G],
+      Cardinal.mk (Profinite.SurjContHom GammaA (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G)))
+        = Cardinal.mk (Profinite.SurjContHom GQ2Profinite
+            (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))) := by
+  intro G _ _ _
+  obtain ⟨Frames, Y, hA, hQ⟩ := tameFrameExhaustion_proven G
+  rw [hA, hQ]
+  congr 1
+  funext φ
+  exact boundaryFramed_counts_equal_currentIntegrated74 (Y φ)
+
+section Acceptance
+
+#print axioms centralCover_realization_proven_fine_from_selfGroupTowerCurrentIntegrated74
+#print axioms boundaryFramed_nonterminal_fourier_branch_currentIntegrated74
+#print axioms finite_surjection_counts_equal_currentIntegrated74
+
+end Acceptance
+
+end Induction
+end Q2Presentation

import Q2Presentation.Induction.CentralCoverFineResiduals

/-!
# Candidate lift-partition cardinality refinements

This sidecar deliberately does **not** prove
`centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinality` for arbitrary independently
chosen obstruction data, cocycle data, and lift fibres.

The manuscript-facing statement is conditional on a genuine exact-image lift
partition, or on a coherent cardinality packet for the same chosen data.
-/

noncomputable section

namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

/-- The cardinality shadow of an actual source lift partition. -/
theorem centralCover_sourceLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p) (S : Type)
    (O : CentralCoverSourceObstructionData B S)
    (Z : CentralCoverSourceCocycleData B)
    (F : CentralCoverSourceLiftFibers B S O Z)
    (P : CentralCoverSourceLiftPartition B S O Z F) :
    CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality B S O Z F :=
  ⟨Nat.card_congr P.bij⟩

/-- Candidate specialization: a genuine bijective lift partition gives the old
cardinality structure for the same `O`, `Z`, and `F`.

This is intentionally conditional on `P`; it is not the overbroad theorem that
quantifies over arbitrary `O`, `Z`, and `F`. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (O : CentralCoverSourceObstructionData B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2))
    (Z : CentralCoverSourceCocycleData B)
    (F : CentralCoverSourceLiftFibers B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z)
    (P : CentralCoverSourceLiftPartition B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z F) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality B
        (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z F) :=
  ⟨centralCover_sourceLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition B _ O Z F P⟩

/-- Manuscript-faithful candidate packet: the obstruction data, cocycle data,
lift fibres, and exact-image partition are selected coherently.

This is the right replacement shape for the broad residual: Section 8 supplies
one genuine decomposition of the candidate boundary-framed surjection set, not a
cardinality theorem for every separately supplied `O`, `Z`, and `F`. -/
structure CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) where
  O : CentralCoverSourceObstructionData B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)
  Z : CentralCoverSourceCocycleData B
  F : CentralCoverSourceLiftFibers B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z
  partition : CentralCoverSourceLiftPartition B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z F

namespace CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData

variable {p : FramedPair} {B : MinimalBlock p}

/-- The cardinality form follows from the coherent candidate partition. -/
def cardinality (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) :
    CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) D.O D.Z D.F :=
  centralCover_sourceLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition B _
    D.O D.Z D.F D.partition

/-- Repackage a coherent candidate partition as source fibre data. -/
def fiberData (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) :
    CentralCoverSourceFiberData B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) D.O D.Z :=
  fiberData_of_fibersPartition B _ D.O D.Z D.F D.partition

/-- Repackage a coherent candidate partition as the source-lift interface used
downstream by the central-cover recursion. -/
def sourceLiftData (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) :
    CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) :=
  sourceLiftData_of_fine B _ D.O D.Z D.fiberData

end CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData

/-- Conditional integration theorem for a genuine candidate lift partition. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
        (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)) :=
  ⟨D.sourceLiftData⟩

/-- Nonempty coherent lift-partition data is sufficient for the candidate
source-lift packet. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData_nonempty
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (hD : Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B)) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
        (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := hD
  exact centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData B D

/-- Coherent cardinality packet: the old cardinality structure, but attached to
the specific candidate obstruction/cocycle/fibre data to which it applies. -/
structure CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) where
  O : CentralCoverSourceObstructionData B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)
  Z : CentralCoverSourceCocycleData B
  F : CentralCoverSourceLiftFibers B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z
  cardinality : CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality B
    (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) O Z F

namespace CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket

variable {p : FramedPair} {B : MinimalBlock p}

/-- Recover the exact-image partition from a coherent cardinality packet.
This is just the existing `liftPartition_of_cardinality`, but with the candidate
source finiteness supplied locally and with no arbitrary-cardinality axiom. -/
def partition (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B) :
    CentralCoverSourceLiftPartition B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) D.O D.Z D.F := by
  haveI : Finite (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) :=
    centralCover_candidateBoundaryFramedSurj_finite
  exact liftPartition_of_cardinality B _ D.O D.Z D.F D.cardinality

/-- A coherent cardinality packet determines a coherent partition packet. -/
def liftPartitionData (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B) :
    CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B where
  O := D.O
  Z := D.Z
  F := D.F
  partition := D.partition

/-- Source-lift data obtained from a coherent cardinality packet. -/
def sourceLiftData (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B) :
    CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
      (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2) :=
  D.liftPartitionData.sourceLiftData

end CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket

/-- Cardinality packet obtained from a genuine coherent partition packet. -/
def centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket_of_liftPartitionData
    {p : FramedPair} {B : MinimalBlock p}
    (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B) :
    CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B where
  O := D.O
  Z := D.Z
  F := D.F
  cardinality := D.cardinality

/-- Refined candidate cardinality statement: there exists a coherently selected
packet, assuming the missing Section 8 integration supplies a genuine coherent
lift partition. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinality_refined_of_liftPartitionData
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (hD : Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B)) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := hD
  exact ⟨centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket_of_liftPartitionData D⟩

/-- Integration theorem from a coherent cardinality packet.  This is the safe
cardinality-to-partition route: the cardinality witness is attached to the same
selected `O`, `Z`, and `F`. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_cardinalityPacket
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
        (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)) :=
  ⟨D.sourceLiftData⟩

/-- Nonempty coherent cardinality data is sufficient for the candidate
source-lift packet. -/
theorem centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_cardinalityPacket_nonempty
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (hD : Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B)) :
    Nonempty
      (CentralCoverSourceLiftData B
        (boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2)) := by
  obtain ⟨D⟩ := hD
  exact centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_cardinalityPacket B D

section Acceptance

#print axioms centralCover_sourceLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition
#print axioms centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinality_of_liftPartition
#print axioms centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_liftPartitionData_nonempty
#print axioms centralCover_candidateLiftPartitionCardinality_refined_of_liftPartitionData
#print axioms centralCover_candidateLiftData_fine_of_cardinalityPacket_nonempty

end Acceptance

end Induction
end Q2Presentation
Why the original axiom should not be kept verbatim

The old axiom says:

lean
∀ O Z F, Nonempty (CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality ... O Z F)

That is too strong because CentralCoverSourceObstructionData and CentralCoverSourceLiftFibers are just data containers. They do not store any map from actual candidate boundary-framed surjections to the unobstructed Sigma type. In particular, centralCover_candidateLiftFibers can fill fibres by the regular torsor Z.Z1, but that only proves fibre nonemptiness/torsor structure. It does not prove that the actual finite set

lean
boundaryFramedSurj boundaryPackage_GammaA p.1 p.2

is in bijection with

lean
Sigma (fun x : {x : O.X // O.o x = 0} => F.Lift x)

for arbitrary O, Z, and F.

The safe replacement is therefore one of these two shapes:

lean
CentralCoverSourceLiftPartition ... O Z F
  → CentralCoverSourceLiftPartitionCardinality ... O Z F

or a coherent packet:

lean
Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionData B)
  → Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateLiftPartitionCardinalityPacket B)

This records the exact missing integration hypothesis: Section 8 must supply the genuine exact-image partition or a cardinality witness tied to the same selected obstruction/cocycle/fibre data. It does not fake a theorem for arbitrary O Z F.

import Q2Presentation.Induction.TargetKmodRSameTowerSemanticClosers

/-!
# Closing the target `K/R` inner-conjugation packet

This sidecar focuses on the current same-tower target residual

* `centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData`.

In the manuscript tower, `R = Phi(K)` and `M = K/R`; hence `K/R` is an
abelian elementary `F_2`-module.  The current API already carries the realised
quotient as


D.KmodR_equivM : (image of K in Y/R) ~= Multiplicative B.M


and `Multiplicative B.M` is abelian because `B.M` is an additive `F_2`-module.
Therefore conjugation by an element of `K` on the subgroup `K/R` is trivial.
The proof below uses only this already-present same-tower bridge and does not
invoke the old retired target quotient/action packets.
-/

noncomputable section

namespace Q2Presentation
namespace Induction

open TargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceProofs
open TargetKmodRSameTowerSemanticClosers

namespace TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed

variable {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
variable (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B)

/-- The image of an element `k : K` in the realised quotient `K/R`. -/
def kClass (k : p.1.Y) (hk : k ∈ D.Ksub) :
    haveI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
    Subgroup.map (QuotientGroup.mk' D.Rsub) D.Ksub := by
  letI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
  exact ⟨QuotientGroup.mk' D.Rsub k, ⟨k, hk, rfl⟩⟩

/-- Ambient quotient conjugation by an element of `K` is the same as subgroup
inner conjugation by its class in `K/R`. -/
theorem quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_kClass_conj
    (k : p.1.Y) (hk : k ∈ D.Ksub)
    (q :
      haveI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
      Subgroup.map (QuotientGroup.mk' D.Rsub) D.Ksub) :
    quotientConjugateKmodRPoint B D k q =
      (kClass B D k hk)⁻¹ * q * (kClass B D k hk) := by
  letI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
  apply Subtype.ext
  simp [quotientConjugateKmodRPoint, kClass]

/-- Since the realised quotient `K/R` is multiplicatively equivalent to the
abelian group `Multiplicative B.M`, inner conjugation by elements of `K` is
trivial on `K/R`. -/
theorem quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_self_of_mem_K
    (k : p.1.Y) (hk : k ∈ D.Ksub)
    (q :
      haveI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
      Subgroup.map (QuotientGroup.mk' D.Rsub) D.Ksub) :
    quotientConjugateKmodRPoint B D k q = q := by
  letI : D.Rsub.Normal := D.Rsub_normal
  have hconj := quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_kClass_conj B D k hk q
  apply D.KmodR_equivM.injective
  rw [hconj]
  simp [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]

/-- The narrow semantic packet `centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData`,
proved from the current `K/R ~= M` bridge. -/
def innerConjugationData :
    CentralCoverTargetKmodRInnerConjugationData B D where
  innerConjugation_trivial := by
    intro k hk q
    exact quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_self_of_mem_K B D k hk q

end TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed

open TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed

/-- Route-ready closure of the current target `K/R` inner-conjugation residual.

This theorem replaces the axiom
`centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData` in downstream target-K/R routes.
Its axiom closure should not contain that residual: the only mathematical input
is the already-carried `D.KmodR_equivM` identifying `K/R` with the abelian module
`M`. -/
theorem centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData_from_KmodR_bridge
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B) :
    Nonempty
      (TargetKmodRSameTowerSemanticClosers.CentralCoverTargetKmodRInnerConjugationData
        B D) :=
  ⟨TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed.innerConjugationData B D⟩

/-- Same-tower quotient-action packet with the inner-conjugation residual closed.

The remaining two target same-tower inputs are exactly the lower projection and
canonical representative-action packets.  No old lower quotient/action packets
are used here. -/
theorem centralCover_targetKmodRQuotientActionData_from_sameTowerSemanticClosers_noInnerResidual
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B) :
    Nonempty (TargetKmodRActionProofs.CentralCoverTargetKmodRQuotientActionData B D) := by
  obtain ⟨P⟩ := centralCover_targetCanonicalLowerProjectionData B D
  obtain ⟨A⟩ := centralCover_targetKmodRCanonicalRepresentativeActionData B D P
  obtain ⟨I⟩ := centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData_from_KmodR_bridge B D
  exact
    ⟨TargetKmodRSameTowerSemanticClosers.quotientActionData_of_semanticClosers
      B D P A I⟩

/-- The decoration-kernel route used by `IntegratedCurrentRouteProofs2`, but with
the inner-conjugation packet discharged by
`centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData_from_KmodR_bridge`. -/
theorem centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_sameTowerSemanticClosers_noInnerResidual
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverTargetDecorationKernelData B D) := by
  obtain ⟨Aquot⟩ :=
    centralCover_targetKmodRQuotientActionData_from_sameTowerSemanticClosers_noInnerResidual
      B D
  let Acompat :
      TargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceProofs.CentralCoverTargetKmodRActionCompatibilityData
        B D :=
    TargetKmodRActionProofs.kmodRActionCompatibilityData_of_quotientAction
      B D Aquot
  let Hinvar :
      TargetDecorationActualDescentProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceData
        B D :=
    TargetDecorationCanonicalInvarianceProofs.canonicalInvarianceData_of_kmodRActionCompatibility
      B D Acompat
  let Hactual :
      TargetDecorationBlockMapActualProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationActualDescentData
        B D :=
    TargetDecorationActualDescentProofs.actualDescentData_of_canonicalInvariance
      B D Hinvar
  let Hblock :
      TargetDecorationKernelProofs.CentralCoverTargetDecorationBlockMapData B D :=
    TargetDecorationBlockMapActualProofs.blockMapData_of_actualDescent
      B D Hactual
  exact
    ⟨TargetDecorationKernelProofs.decorationKernelData_of_blockMap
      B D Hblock⟩

section Acceptance

#print axioms TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed.quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_kClass_conj
#print axioms TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed.quotientConjugateKmodRPoint_eq_self_of_mem_K
#print axioms TargetKmodRInnerConjugationClosed.innerConjugationData
#print axioms centralCover_targetKmodRInnerConjugationData_from_KmodR_bridge
#print axioms centralCover_targetKmodRQuotientActionData_from_sameTowerSemanticClosers_noInnerResidual
#print axioms centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_from_sameTowerSemanticClosers_noInnerResidual

end Acceptance

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

I inspected the two unzipped source trees directly and used the manuscript/plan as a yardstick for what a completed proof must close: a presentation_correct : Nonempty (GammaA ≅ GQ2Profinite) theorem via finite epimorphism counts and profinite reconstruction, with final acceptance requiring no sorry and no custom mathematical axioms beyond standard Lean/classical axioms.

I could not independently rebuild either project in this environment because lean/lake are not installed here. The comparison below is therefore based on static source inspection plus the build/audit logs included in the Q2 snapshot.

Bottom line

Q2 v428 is further along in integration/theorem assembly. It has a top-level presentation_correct theorem wired through GammaA, finite surjection count equality, and reconstruction, and the included audit log reports a successful build of Q2Presentation.PresentationCorrect. It also has zero visible sorry/admit occurrences after stripping comments and strings.

GQ2 is closer to an unconditional completed proof by proof-debt metric. It still has visible sorrys in decisive places, including the main presentation/count endpoints, so it is not as integrated. But its remaining debt is much smaller and more coherently isolated: 15 centralized axioms plus 11 sorrys, versus Q2’s 660 axiom declarations overall and 41 project axioms in the final theorem’s printed axiom closure.

So the answer differs by metric:

Metric	Further along
Has capstone theorem assembled	Q2 v428

Closer to a trustworthy axiom-free completion	GQ2, in my judgment
Closer as a compiled conditional scaffold	Q2 v428

One technical caveat: the Q2 zip manifest says lakefile.lean is included, but the extracted Q2 root I inspected did not contain a lakefile.lean; it had Q2Presentation.lean, lake-manifest.json, lean-toolchain, PROGRESS.md, and reference/. That does not invalidate the source comparison, but it means I would not treat the zip itself as a directly rebuildable Lake project without restoring the missing project file. The included Q2 audit logs nevertheless report a successful build of the top-level theorem in the original environment.

GQ2: what is genuinely formalized, and what remains open

GQ2 is not just a README/progress shell. It has substantial Lean development around the actual manuscript architecture: candidate group construction, finite quotient/admissibility machinery, word infrastructure, Fox/Heisenberg and section-level files, and a visible attempt to track classical literature assumptions in one file.

The strongest positive point is that its assumptions are centralized. All 15 axiom declarations occur in GQ2/Foundations/Axioms.lean, and they are recognizable as high-level classical inputs: absolute Galois group topological finite generation, marked maximal pro-2/Demushkin input, local Euler/Tate/Hilbert-type facts, tame quotient facts, and related local arithmetic/lifting ingredients. That is a healthier proof-audit shape than scattering hundreds of local axioms across the induction.

But GQ2 still has decisive sorrys. The most important ones are:

GQ2/GammaA.lean: main_presentation_literal : Nonempty (ContinuousMulEquiv GammaA AbsGalQ2) is by sorry.

GQ2/Statement.lean: main_surjection_count, the finite surjection/admissible count equality, is by sorry.

GQ2/SectionNine.lean: thm_4_2, the boundary-framed exact-image equality, is by sorry.

GQ2/SectionEight.lean: prop_8_9, a closed recursion theorem, is by sorry.

GQ2/SectionSix.lean: local determinant/unramified pieces such as lemma_6_17_dim, lemma_6_17_vanish, and prop_6_18_unramified are by sorry.

Those are not cosmetic gaps. They sit exactly where the manuscript/plan identifies the hardest parts: local Q₂ input, determinant comparison, boundary-framed exact-image counts, and finite target induction. The plan explicitly treats the exact-image semantics and finite epimorphism equivalence as load-bearing, and the boundary/local marked Demushkin package as a high-risk bottleneck.

plan_4turns

Assessment of GQ2: mathematically cleaner and probably closer to an axiom-free proof if the remaining named obligations can be filled, but not currently a closed formal proof.

Q2 v428: what is genuinely formalized, and what remains open

Q2 v428 is much more integrated. It implements the finite-quotient-first architecture from the plan in a very literal way:

Foundation/ contains the generator/word/ω₂/two-core/admissibility infrastructure.

Candidate/ contains the admissible index/category, GammaA, finite factorization, and epimorphism semantics.

Boundary/, Local/, Lifting/, Quadratic/, and Induction/ contain the later proof architecture.

PresentationCorrect.lean assembles the final theorem:

lean
theorem presentation_correct : Nonempty (GammaA ≅ GQ2Profinite)

via profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts, tfg_GammaA, and surj_counts_equal.

This is meaningfully further than a skeleton. For example, Q2 has the planned exact-image/epimorphism structure: maps out of GammaA to finite targets are controlled by exact images and admissible markings, which is the right formal shape for Proposition 2.3 and later boundary-framed induction. The earlier plan identifies this exact-image route as central because the naive ambient non-surjective statement is false. 

plan_4turns

The serious problem is that Q2 obtains this closure by axiom packaging. Static inspection found 660 axiom declarations spread across 169 files, mostly in Q2Presentation/Induction. More importantly, the included audit log for #print axioms presentation_correct shows that the final theorem depends on 41 project axioms, including:

Q2Presentation.gq2_tame_quotient

Q2Presentation.labute_GQ2_maxPro2_marked

Q2Presentation.Local.q2_local_tate_duality

Q2Presentation.Local.q2_local_euler_poincare

multiple central-cover affine/fourier/local-Arf/Hilbert/Evens interface axioms

multiple Section 7 Frattini-trivial elementary-stage data axioms

several candidate wild-row manuscript data axioms

terminal marked pro-2/local CFT compatibility axioms

route/action representative axioms

These are not merely tiny API gaps. They cover large parts of the hard mathematical content: local Tate duality, local Euler characteristic, marked Labute/Demushkin input, Gauss/Hilbert/Evens comparison, affine lifting/fiber equivalences, candidate wild-row data, and terminal local class-field compatibility. The plan’s own acceptance criterion says the final theorem should not depend on custom mathematical axioms, so Q2’s current theorem is best described as a compiled conditional theorem over a large axiom interface, not as a completed formal proof. 

plan_4turns

Assessment of Q2: much further as a full proof scaffold and capstone integration, but farther from an unconditional proof than it first appears because the final theorem still imports a large axiom frontier.

Which is “closer to completion”?

I would separate the answer into two notions.

Closer to a working end-to-end formalization artifact: Q2 v428. It has the final theorem assembled, no visible sorry, and an included successful build/audit log for the capstone file.

Closer to a completed formal proof in the usual Lean/mathlib sense: GQ2. It has fewer total assumptions, the axioms are centralized, and the remaining visible gaps are named theorem gaps rather than hundreds of scattered axiomatic data nodes. Even though some of GQ2’s remaining sorrys are hard, the proof-debt surface is much smaller and more auditable.

My forced single verdict would be:

Q2 is further along, but GQ2 is closer to a clean completed proof.

Q2 looks like the better “integration branch”; GQ2 looks like the better “proof-trust branch.”

Practical implication

If the goal is to submit or certify a completed Lean proof, neither project is there yet. The next decisive step differs:

For Q2, the priority is not more top-level wiring. It already has that. The priority is to deaxiomatize the 41 project axioms in the presentation_correct closure, especially the local Q₂ inputs, central-cover/Fourier/lifting interfaces, terminal marked pro-2 compatibility, and candidate wild-row data.

For GQ2, the priority is to eliminate the 11 visible sorrys, especially main_surjection_count, thm_4_2, prop_8_9, and the Section 6 local determinant/unramified lemmas. Once those are gone, its much smaller centralized axiom ledger becomes the remaining target.

So: use Q2 as the map of the full proof pipeline; use GQ2 as the more credible base for an eventually axiom-free proof audit.

and keeps quotient/equivalence provenance to the current module-level API. The second represents the determinant pushout construction for the same tower: the scalar central pushouts K_λ, their square maps, C-naturality, annihilation on T, and polar-radical containment.

from the existing Quadratic.Extraspecial.extraspecialForm API.

annihilates_T :

What should remain as the honest §7 semantic floor

These are not honestly provable from the current API alone:

The reason is structural. The current MinimalBlockPreheadTowerData is already module-level: it has abstract R, M, T, C, actR, and actM. It does not contain enough finite-group quotient data to prove that actR and actM are induced by conjugation in Y/K, nor does it contain the actual class-two group extension K_λ.

So the sidecar refines them into semantic packets instead of pretending to close them:

lean

    {chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p}
    (G : MinimalBlockGroupTowerSemanticData p chief) :
    Nonempty

This is a better residual boundary than the current eight names because it tracks the actual manuscript construction rather than scattering projections of it across unrelated packets.

Why these are the right reductions

The target K/R route is reduced to the same-tower semantic facts already present in the API: the canonical lower projection Y → C = Y/K, the conjugation action on K/R, and the fact that changing representatives by an element of K acts trivially on K/R. This is exactly the Section 7 tower R ≤ K ≤ Y and the Section 8 target action transport needed for central covers.

The simple-head packets are reduced to irreducibility/nontrivial-action/hyperplane/invariant-character packets. That matches the manuscript’s simple-head lemma: M/T ≅ V, T = rad M, and the non-scalar simple head controls the invariant functional behavior.

The extraspecial packets are reduced to the actual map plus naturality, annihilation on T, and radical containment. Those are the pieces used in the Section 7 determinant pushout argument before descending to the simple head.

The candidate packets are reduced to a scalar obstruction evaluator split into values plus linearity, and a Frattini affine-fibre coordinate equivalence split into maps plus inverse laws. That mirrors Section 8’s obstruction separation and affine-fibre parameterization rather than keeping an opaque exact-image equivalence packet.

This matches the current project note that the old candidate exact-image packet was too strong and should be split into quotient-frame compatibility, R-lift obstruction, and Frattini exact-image equivalence.

Main sidecar theorems

The first small theorem is a direct constructor for the §8 target packet. It does not close centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData by itself, but it identifies the exact manuscript-shaped proof obligation: the decoration map kills Ksub.

lean
theorem centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_of_Ksub_le_thetaY_ker
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetSubgroupConcreteData B)
    (hθ : D.Ksub ≤ p.1.thetaY.ker) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverTargetDecorationKernelData B D) :=
  ⟨{ Ksub_le_thetaY_ker := hθ }⟩

So packet 2 should be replaced by the route theorem:

centralCover_targetDecorationKernelData_of_Ksub_le_thetaY_ker

and the residual should become exactly:

D.Ksub ≤ p.1.thetaY.ker

The second small theorem packages the already-split candidate semantic components without touching the coarse aggregate:

lean
theorem centralCover_candidateCanonicalSemanticResidualData_of_components
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (D : CentralCoverTargetCanonicalQuotientTowerData B)
    (Q : CentralCoverCandidateActualTopToMiddlePostcompData B D.tower)
    (E : CentralCoverCandidateMiddleExactImageObstructionEvaluationData B D.tower)
    (F : CentralCoverCandidateTopExactImageAffineFiberEquivData B D.tower E) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverCandidateCanonicalSemanticResidualData B D) :=
  ⟨{ actualPostcomp := Q, middleEvaluation := E, topAffineFiberEquiv := F }⟩

A. Section 7: carry the realized subgroup tower, not just abstract modules

The current MinimalBlock abstraction has R, M, T, C, actions, and pushout forms, but it has lost the realized finite subgroups R = Φ(K) ≤ K ≤ Y. That makes several downstream packets unprovable from the current fields alone. Add a semantic realized packet:

structure MinimalBlockRealizedSubgroupData (p : FramedPair)
    (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p) where

  K_fourth_power : ∀ k : Ksub, (k : p.1.Y) ^ 4 = 1

B. Section 8 target tower and proper-image cardinality

Strengthen the target tower so the quotient maps know they preserve the boundary-framed structure. The present CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData has quotient maps and cardinal fields, but not enough compatibility to prove the exact-image child cardinalities honestly.

Use a concrete quotient-tower structure:

lean
structure CentralCoverConcreteQuotientTowerData {p : FramedPair}
    (B : MinimalBlock p) where
  Rsub Ksub : Subgroup p.1.Y
  Rsub_normal : Rsub.Normal
  Ksub_normal : Ksub.Normal
  Rsub_le_Ksub : Rsub ≤ Ksub
  Ksub_le_LY : Ksub ≤ p.1.LY

  middleTarget : BoundaryFramedTarget
  lowerTarget : BoundaryFramedTarget
  middleFrame : BoundaryFrame middleTarget
  lowerFrame : BoundaryFrame lowerTarget

  topToMiddle : p.1.Y →* middleTarget.Y
  middleToLower : middleTarget.Y →* lowerTarget.Y
  topToLower : p.1.Y →* lowerTarget.Y

  topToMiddle_surj : Function.Surjective topToMiddle
  middleToLower_surj : Function.Surjective middleToLower
  topToLower_surj : Function.Surjective topToLower

  topToMiddle_ker : topToMiddle.ker = Rsub
  topToLower_ker : topToLower.ker = Ksub
  quotient_commutes : middleToLower.comp topToMiddle = topToLower

  topToMiddle_qY :
    ∀ y, middleTarget.qYMap (topToMiddle y) = p.1.qYMap y
  middleToLower_qY :
    ∀ y, lowerTarget.qYMap (middleToLower y) = middleTarget.qYMap y

  kernel_equiv_M :
    middleToLower.ker ≃* Multiplicative B.M

Then reassemble the existing target tower:

lean

    CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B := ...

For centralCover_elementaryProperImageCardinalityData, define:

  Nat.card (T.middleToLower.ker.subgroupOf i.1)

Then prove:

lean

    (i : CentralCoverElementaryProperExactImageSubgroups T) :
    2 * kernelPartCard i ≤ Nat.card B.M

theorem elementaryProper_child_measure_eq
    (i : CentralCoverElementaryProperExactImageSubgroups T) :
    framedMeasure (elementaryExactImageChild T.middleChild i.1 i.2.2.1) =
      kernelPartCard i * framedMeasure T.lowerChild

theorem centralCover_elementaryProperImageCardinalityData_from_exactSequence
    {p : FramedPair} (B : MinimalBlock p)
    (T : CentralCoverElementaryQuotientTowerData B)
    (D : CentralCoverConcreteQuotientTowerData B)
    (hT : D.toElementaryQuotientTowerData = T) :
    Nonempty (CentralCoverElementaryProperImageCardinalityData B T)

The proof is finite exact-sequence arithmetic: for J < B mapping onto C, use
1 → J ∩ ker(B→C) → J ∩ L_B → L_C → 1; if J ∩ ker = ker, surjectivity to C forces J = ⊤, contradicting properness, so the subgroup index is at least 2.

  classical

  intro f
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · refine

      exact ⟨γ, rfl⟩
  · intro γ
    exact hframe f γ

end Induction
end Q2Presentation

lean
structure MinimalBlockGroupTowerData (p : FramedPair)
    (chief : NonScalarChiefFactor p) where
  Ksub : Subgroup p.1.Y
  Ksub_normal : Ksub.Normal
  Ksub_le_upper : Ksub ≤ chief.upper
  Ksub_le_LY : Ksub ≤ p.1.LY
  KS_eq_upper : Ksub ⊔ chief.lower = chief.upper
  minimal_K :
    ∀ K' : Subgroup p.1.Y,
      K'.Normal →
      K' ≤ chief.upper →
      K' ⊔ chief.lower = chief.upper →
      Ksub ≤ K'
  Rsub : Subgroup p.1.Y
  Rsub_eq_frattini : Rsub = Subgroup.map Ksub.subtype (frattini Ksub)
  Rsub_normal : Rsub.Normal
  Rsub_le_Ksub : Rsub ≤ Ksub
  Rsub_card_gt_one : 1 < Nat.card Rsub
  R_central_elementary : True -- split this into concrete fields if needed
  KmodR_equivM :
    haveI : Rsub.Normal := Rsub_normal
    Subgroup.map (QuotientGroup.mk' Rsub) Ksub ≃* Multiplicative
      (MinimalBlockRawModuleData p chief).M

The last field cannot literally mention a separately chosen MinimalBlockRawModuleData unless you thread that data through; a better implementation is to make MinimalBlockGroupTowerData produce the raw module data, not depend on it.

centralCover_candidateConcreteExactImageData	mixed: boundary easy, obstruction/frattini hard	no semantic obstruction/equivalence yet
minimalBlock_rawModuleData	larger construction	need K, R=Φ(K), M=K/R, T from §7
minimalBlock_rawActionData	small after group tower	induced conjugation actions
minimalBlock_headNontrivialData	small after group tower	identify M/T with chief factor
minimalBlock_dualInvariantVanishesData	medium/larger	prove (Mᵛ)^C = 0
minimalBlock_rawInvariantCharacterData	medium/larger	needs scalar lower-layer invariant dual
minimalBlock_preheadCentralPushoutSelfDualityData	larger construction	actual central pushout square/polar map
minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialNaturalityData	small after pushout	conjugation naturality
minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialDiagonalZeroData	medium	`qλ
minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialPolarZeroData	medium	bλ(T,M)=0
minimalBlock_preheadExtraspecialRadicalContainmentData	hard	nonsingularity/radical equals T

The highest-yield move is therefore not to attack the final axioms one by one. First put the actual subgroup tower into the §7 API. That single construction should unlock the target quotient/proper-image packets and prevent the candidate Frattini proof from floating over an arbitrary, under-specified T.
